name: Add file sharing preference
description: Modifies sharing permissions for an existing google drive file, granting a specified role to a user, group, domain, or 'anyone'.

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of grantee for the permission.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Domain to grant permission to (e.g., 'example.com'). Required if 'type' is 'domain'.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Unique identifier of the file to update sharing settings for.

email_address.title: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the user or group. Required if 'type' is 'user' or 'group'.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: 'drive#permission'.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role granted by this permission.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the grantee.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain to which this permission refers.

deleted.title: Deleted

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user or group to which this permission refers.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user or group to which this permission refers.

expirationTime.title: Expiration Time
expirationTime.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time).

role.title: Role
role.description: The role granted by this permission.

inheritedFrom.title: Inherited From
inheritedFrom.description: The ID of the item from which this permission is inherited.

allowFileDiscovery.title: Allow File Discovery

role.title: Role

inheritedFrom.title: Inherited From

human_description: This action updates the sharing settings of a Google Drive file. It allows the assignment of specific permissions to users, groups, or domains, enhancing collaborative capabilities. Before using this action, first find the target file using GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE or GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES to get the correct file_id.

name: Modify email labels
description: Adds and/or removes specified gmail labels for a message; ensure `message id` and all `label ids` are valid (use 'listlabels' for custom label ids).

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

message_id.title: Message Id
message_id.description: Immutable ID of the message to modify (e.g., from 'fetchEmails' or 'fetchMessagesByThreadId').

add_label_ids.title: Add Label Ids
add_label_ids.description: Label IDs to add. For custom labels, obtain IDs via 'listLabels'. System labels (e.g., 'INBOX', 'SPAM') can also be used.

remove_label_ids.title: Remove Label Ids
remove_label_ids.description: Label IDs to remove. For custom labels, obtain IDs via 'listLabels'. System labels can also be used.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Full `Message` resource with updated labels.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows you to add or remove labels from a specific email message in Gmail. By providing the user's ID and the message ID, you can modify the labels associated with that email, helping to organize or categorize messages as needed.

name: Add or remove to customer list

name: Add Sheet to Spreadsheet
description: Adds a new sheet (worksheet) to a spreadsheet. use this tool to create a new tab within an existing google sheet, optionally specifying its title, index, size, and other properties.

hidden.title: Hidden

sheetId.title: Sheet Id

Supports three sheet types: GRID (standard spreadsheet), OBJECT (chart on its own sheet), and DATA_SOURCE (connected to BigQuery). Users can define properties such as sheet name, tab color, grid dimensions, and chart/data source configurations.

name: Aggregate Column Data
description: Searches for rows where a specific column matches a value and performs mathematical operations on data from another column.

input_parameters.title: AggregateColumnDataRequest

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: The mathematical operation to perform on the target column values.

sheet_name.title: Sheet Name
sheet_name.description: The name of the specific sheet within the spreadsheet.

search_value.title: Search Value
search_value.description: The exact value to search for in the search column. Case-sensitive by default.

search_column.title: Search Column
search_column.description: The column to search in. Can be a letter (e.g., 'A', 'B') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Region', 'Department').

target_column.title: Target Column
target_column.description: The column to aggregate data from. Can be a letter (e.g., 'C', 'D') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Sales', 'Revenue').

case_sensitive.title: Case Sensitive
case_sensitive.description: Whether the search should be case-sensitive.

has_header_row.title: Has Header Row
has_header_row.description: Whether the first row contains column headers. If True, column names can be used for search_column and target_column.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet.

percentage_total.title: Percentage Total
percentage_total.description: For percentage operation, the total value to calculate percentage against. If not provided, uses sum of all values in target column.

input_parameters.description: Request to search for rows matching a column value and aggregate data from another column.

result.title: Result
result.description: The result of the aggregation operation.

search_details.title: Search Details
search_details.description: Details about the search performed, including column names and values.

values_processed.title: Values Processed
values_processed.description: The actual numeric values that were processed (useful for debugging).

matching_rows_count.title: Matching Rows Count
matching_rows_count.description: Number of rows that matched the search criteria.

processed_values_count.title: Processed Values Count
processed_values_count.description: Number of non-empty numeric values that were processed.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action searches for specific rows in a Google Sheets spreadsheet based on a given column's value. It then performs selected mathematical operations on another column's data to provide aggregated results.

sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet to append rows or columns to.

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet.

name: Batch get spreadsheet
description: Retrieves data from specified cell ranges in a google spreadsheet; ensure the spreadsheet has at least one worksheet and any explicitly referenced sheet names in ranges exist.

ranges.title: Ranges
ranges.description: A list of cell ranges in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2', 'A1:C5') from which to retrieve data. If this list is omitted or empty, all data from the first sheet of the spreadsheet will be fetched. A range can specify a sheet name (e.g., 'Sheet2!A:A'); if no sheet name is provided in a range string (e.g., 'A1:B2'), it defaults to the first sheet.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which data will be retrieved.

spreadsheet_data.title: Spreadsheet Data
spreadsheet_data.description: A dictionary containing the retrieved data. It includes the `spreadsheetId`. The `valueRanges` key holds the cell data: this is a list of value range objects if specific `ranges` were requested, or a single value range object representing all data from the first sheet if `ranges` were omitted.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves data from specific cell ranges in a Google Spreadsheet. Users can specify which ranges to get the values from, and the action handles the necessary authentication and API requests.

mediaItemResults.title: Media Item Results
mediaItemResults.description: List of media items retrieved. May include status for failed items.

error.title: Error

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Batch update spreadsheet
description: Updates a specified range in a google sheet with given values, or appends them as new rows if `first cell location` is omitted; ensure the target sheet exists and the spreadsheet contains at least one worksheet.

input_parameters.title: BatchUpdateRequest

values.title: Values
values.description: A 2D list of cell values. Each inner list represents a row. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans. Ensure columns are properly aligned across rows.

sheet_name.title: Sheet Name
sheet_name.description: The name of the specific sheet within the spreadsheet to update.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet to be updated.

valueInputOption.title: Value Input Option
valueInputOption.description: How input data is interpreted. 'USER_ENTERED': Values parsed as if typed by a user (e.g., strings may become numbers/dates, formulas are calculated); recommended for formulas. 'RAW': Values stored as-is without parsing (e.g., '123' stays string, '=SUM(A1:B1)' stays string).

first_cell_location.title: First Cell Location
first_cell_location.description: The starting cell for the update range, specified in A1 notation (e.g., 'A1', 'B2'). The update will extend from this cell to the right and down, based on the provided values. If omitted, values are appended to the sheet.

includeValuesInResponse.title: Include Values In Response
includeValuesInResponse.description: If set to True, the response will include the updated values from the spreadsheet.

input_parameters.description: Request to update a range of cells or append new rows in a Google Sheet with specified values.

spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: Contains details of the update operation, such as spreadsheet ID, updated range, and counts of updated rows, columns, and cells. This is included if `includeValuesInResponse` was true in the request and the update was successful.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Write values to a single range in a Google Sheet (or append as new rows). NOTE: This tool handles ONE range per call - for multiple ranges, make separate calls. Requires 'sheet_name' and 'values' parameters (the Google API's native 'data' array format is NOT supported).

name: Batch Update Values by Data Filter
description: Tool to update values in ranges matching data filters. use when you need to update specific data in a google sheet based on criteria rather than fixed cell ranges.

values.title: Values
values.description: The data to be written. A two-dimensional array of values that will be written to the range. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans. If the range is larger than the values array, the excess cells will not be changed. If the values array is larger than the range, the excess values will be ignored.

dataFilter.title: Data Filter

a1Range.title: A1 Range
a1Range.description: The A1 notation of the range to update.

gridRange.title: GridRange

sheetId.title: Sheet Id
sheetId.description: The sheet this range is on.

endRowIndex.title: End Row Index
endRowIndex.description: The end row (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

startRowIndex.title: Start Row Index
startRowIndex.description: The start row (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

endColumnIndex.title: End Column Index
endColumnIndex.description: The end column (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

startColumnIndex.title: Start Column Index
startColumnIndex.description: The start column (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

gridRange.description: Selects data within the range described by a GridRange. This field is optional. If specified, the dataFilter selects data within the specified grid range.

developerMetadataLookup.title: DeveloperMetadataLookup

metadataId.title: Metadata Id
metadataId.description: The ID of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified ID.

visibility.title: Visibility
visibility.description: The visibility of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified visibility.

metadataKey.title: Metadata Key
metadataKey.description: The key of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified key.

locationType.title: Location Type
locationType.description: The type of location this object is looking for. Valid values are ROW, COLUMN, and SHEET.

metadataValue.title: Metadata Value
metadataValue.description: The value of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified value.

metadataLocation.title: DeveloperMetadataLocation

sheetId.title: Sheet Id
sheetId.description: The ID of the sheet the location is on.

spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: True if the metadata location is the spreadsheet itself.

locationType.title: Location Type
locationType.description: The type of location this object represents. This field is read-only.

dimensionRange.title: Dimension Range
dimensionRange.description: A range along a single dimension on a sheet. All indexes are 0-based. Indexes are half open: the start index is inclusive and the end index is exclusive. Missing indexes indicate the range is unbounded on that side.

metadataLocation.description: The location of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata in the specified location.

locationMatchingStrategy.title: Location Matching Strategy
locationMatchingStrategy.description: Determines how this lookup matches the location. If this field is specified as EXACT, then the lookup requires an exact match of the specified locationType, metadataKey, and metadataValue. If this field is specified as INTERSECTING, then the lookup considers all metadata that intersects the specified locationType, and then filters that metadata by the specified key and value. If this field is unspecified, it is treated as EXACT.

developerMetadataLookup.description: Matches the data against the developer metadata that's associated with the dimensions. The developer metadata should be created with the location type set to either ROW or COLUMN and the visibility set to DOCUMENT.

dataFilter.description: The data filter describing the criteria to select cells for update.

majorDimension.title: Major Dimension
majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values. The default value is ROWS.

data.title: Data
data.description: The new values to apply to the spreadsheet. If more than one range is matched by the specified DataFilter the specified values are applied to all of those ranges.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

valueInputOption.title: Value Input Option
valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted.

includeValuesInResponse.title: Include Values In Response
includeValuesInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

responseValueRenderOption.title: Response Value Render Option
responseValueRenderOption.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE.

responseDateTimeRenderOption.title: Response Date Time Render Option
responseDateTimeRenderOption.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. This is ignored if responseValueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER.

spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: True if the metadata location is the spreadsheet itself.

locationType.title: Location Type
locationType.description: The type of location this object represents. This field is read-only.

dimensionRange.title: Dimension Range
dimensionRange.description: A range along a single dimension on a sheet. All indexes are 0-based. Indexes are half open: the start index is inclusive and the end index is exclusive. Missing indexes indicate the range is unbounded on that side.

metadataLocation.description: The location of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata in the specified location.

locationMatchingStrategy.title: Location Matching Strategy
locationMatchingStrategy.description: Determines how this lookup matches the location. If this field is specified as EXACT, then the lookup requires an exact match of the specified locationType, metadataKey, and metadataValue. If this field is specified as INTERSECTING, then the lookup considers all metadata that intersects the specified locationType, and then filters that metadata by the specified key and value. updatedData.title: Updated Data

name: Insert Calendar into List
description: Inserts an existing calendar into the user's calendar list.

color_rgb_format.description: Whether to use the foregroundColor and backgroundColor fields to write the calendar colors (RGB). If this feature is used, the index-based colorId field will be set to the best matching option automatically. Optional. The default is False.

This action updates an existing calendar entry for the user.

name: Delete Calendar
description: Deletes a secondary calendar. use calendars.clear for clearing all events on primary calendars.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

message.title: Message
message.description: Message indicating the successful deletion of the calendar.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action permanently deletes a secondary calendar from Google Calendar. You must be the owner or have delete permissions on the calendar. Primary calendars cannot be deleted. To find which calendars you can delete, use the List Calendars action and look for calendars where your accessRole is 'owner'.

name: Update Calendar

name: Clear Basic Filter
description: Tool to clear the basic filter from a sheet. use when you need to remove an existing basic filter from a specific sheet within a google spreadsheet.

sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet on which the basic filter should be cleared.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet.

error.title: Error

human_description: This action clears any existing basic filter from the specified sheet of a Google Spreadsheet. It allows users to reset the sheet's filter settings for better data visibility.

name: Clear Calendar
description: Clears a primary calendar. this operation deletes all events associated with the primary calendar of an account.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the `calendarList.list` method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "`primary`" keyword.

message.title: Message
message.description: Success message indicating the calendar was cleared.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action clears all events from the user's primary calendar. It is a destructive operation that permanently removes all calendar events for the specified calendar.

name: Clear tasks
description: Permanently clears all completed tasks from a specified google tasks list; this action is destructive and idempotent.

tasklist.title: Tasklist
tasklist.description: The identifier of the task list from which to clear completed tasks. Use '@default' for the default task list.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: A dictionary indicating the outcome of the clear operation. On successful completion (HTTP 204), it returns `{'status': 'success'}`. In case of errors, it may contain a 'message' with details from the API response.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Clear spreadsheet values
description: Clears cell content (preserving formatting and notes) from a specified a1 notation range in a google spreadsheet; the range must correspond to an existing sheet and cells.

range.title: Range
range.description: The A1 notation of the range to clear values from (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2', 'MySheet!C:C', or 'A1:D5'). If the sheet name is omitted (e.g., 'A1:B2'), the operation applies to the first visible sheet.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which to clear values. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet.

clearedRange.title: Cleared Range
clearedRange.description: The A1 notation of the range that was actually cleared, which might differ from the requested range if it was adjusted to fit the sheet's dimensions.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet that was updated.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action clears the content from a specified range in a Google Spreadsheet, using the provided A1 notation. The formatting and notes within the cells remain intact, making it ideal for resetting data without losing layout specifics.

name: Copy Google Document
description: Tool to create a copy of an existing google document. use this to duplicate a document, for example, when using an existing document as a template. the copied document will have a default title (e.g., 'copy of [original title]') if no new title is provided, and will be placed in the user's root google drive folder.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title for the new copied document. If not provided, the title will be 'Copy of [original document's title]'.

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Creates a copy of the specified Google Document. If a new title is not provided, the copy will default to 'Copy of [original title]'. This action places the new document in the user's root Google Drive folder.

name: Create Chart in Google Sheets
description: Create a chart in a google sheets spreadsheet using the specified data range and chart type.

title.title: Title
title.description: Optional title for the chart.

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet within the spreadsheet where the chart will be created. Defaults to 0 (first sheet).

chart_type.title: Chart Type
chart_type.description: The type of chart to create. Supported types: BAR, LINE, AREA, COLUMN, SCATTER, COMBO, STEPPED_AREA.

data_range.title: Data Range
data_range.description: The range of data to use for the chart in A1 notation (e.g., 'A1:C10').

background_red.title: Background Red
background_red.description: Red component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet where the chart will be created.

background_blue.title: Background Blue
background_blue.description: Blue component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

legend_position.title: Legend Position
legend_position.description: Position of the chart legend. Options: BOTTOM_LEGEND, TOP_LEGEND, LEFT_LEGEND, RIGHT_LEGEND, NO_LEGEND.

background_green.title: Background Green
background_green.description: Green component of chart background color (0.0-1.0). If not specified, uses default.

chart_id.title: Chart Id
chart_id.description: The ID of the created chart.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the chart.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a chart in a specified Google Sheets spreadsheet. Users can define the type of chart and the data range from which the chart will draw its data.

anchor.description: A JSON string representing the region of the document to which the comment is anchored (e.g., {'type': 'line', 'line': 12}).

content.title: Content
content.description: The plain text content of the comment.

quoted_file_content_value.title: Quoted File Content Value
examples: This is the text to quote.

quoted_file_content_mime_type.title: Quoted File Content Mime Type
quoted_file_content_mime_type.description: The MIME type of the quoted content.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource type, always 'drive#comment'.

anchor.title: Anchor
anchor.description: The region of the document to which the comment is anchored, if any.

author.title: Author
author.description: The user who created the comment.

content.title: Content
content.description: The plain text content of the comment.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Whether the comment has been deleted.

createdTime.title: Created Time
createdTime.description: The time the comment was created.

modifiedTime.title: Modified Time
modifiedTime.description: The time the comment was last modified.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new comment on a specified file in Google Drive. It allows users to provide feedback or notes related to the document by adding plain text comments.

description: Createcustomerlist tool will create a customer list in google ads.

description.description: Description of the customer list.

resource_name.title: Resource Name
resource_name.description: Resource name of the created customer list.

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

name: Create a document
description: Creates a new google docs document using the provided title as filename and inserts the initial text at the beginning if non-empty, returning the document's id and metadata (excluding body content).

text.title: Text
examples: This document outlines the key findings...
text.description: Initial text content to insert at the beginning of the new document.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title for the new document, used as its filename in Google Drive.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: Unique ID of the newly created document, required for subsequent operations.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Detailed API response metadata about the created document (e.g., title, revision ID), excluding the document's body content.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new Google Docs document with a specified title and inserts initial text into it if provided. Upon successful creation, it returns the document's ID and other metadata, excluding the body content.

name: Create Document Markdown
description: Creates a new google docs document, optionally initializing it with a title and content provided as markdown text.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title for the new Google Docs document.

markdown_text.title: Markdown Text
markdown_text.description: The initial content for the document, formatted as Markdown. Supports various Markdown elements including headings, lists (nested lists are not supported), tables, images (via publicly accessible URLs), blockquotes, code blocks, and text formatting (bold, italic, etc.). If an empty string is provided, the document will be created with only the title.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The unique identifier of the newly created Google Docs document.

request_data.title: Request Data
request_data.description: Google Docs API request objects generated from `markdown_text` for `batchUpdate`; null or empty if `markdown_text` was not provided or empty.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: JSON response from Google Docs API after initial document creation with title, before Markdown content is applied.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a Google Docs document. You can set a title and provide initial content in Markdown format, which will be converted to the document's body.

name: Create Shared Drive
description: Tool to create a new shared drive. use when you need to programmatically create a new shared drive for collaboration or storage.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of this shared drive.

hidden.title: Hidden
hidden.description: Whether the shared drive is hidden from default view.

themeId.title: Theme Id
themeId.description: The ID of the theme from which the background image and color will be set. The set of possible driveThemes can be retrieved from a drive.about.get response. When not specified on a drive.drives.create request, a random theme is chosen from which the background image and color are set. This is a write-only field; it can only be set on requests that don't set colorRgb or backgroundImageFile.

colorRgb.title: Color Rgb
colorRgb.description: The color of this shared drive as an RGB hex string. It can only be set on a drive.drives.update request that does not set themeId.

requestId.title: Request Id
requestId.description: An ID, such as a random UUID, which uniquely identifies this user's request for idempotent creation of a shared drive. A repeated request by the same user and with the same request ID will avoid creating duplicates by attempting to create the same shared drive. If the shared drive already exists a 409 error will be returned.

backgroundImageFile.title: BackgroundImageFile

id.title: Id
id.description: The ID of an image file in Google Drive to use for the background.

width.title: Width
width.description: The width of the cropped image in the range: 0.0 <= width <= 1.0.

xCoordinate.title: X Coordinate
xCoordinate.description: The X coordinate of the cropped image in the range: 0.0 <= xCoordinate <= 1.0.

yCoordinate.title: Y Coordinate
yCoordinate.description: The Y coordinate of the cropped image in the range: 0.0 <= yCoordinate <= 1.0.

backgroundImageFile.description: An image file and cropping parameters from which a background image for this shared drive is set. This is a write only field; it can only be set on drive.drives.update requests that don't set themeId. When specified, all fields of the backgroundImageFile must be set.

id.title: Id
id.description: The ID of this shared drive.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#drive".

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of this shared drive.

hidden.title: Hidden
hidden.description: Whether the shared drive is hidden from default view.

themeId.title: Theme Id
themeId.description: The ID of the theme from which the background image and color will be set.

colorRgb.title: Color Rgb
colorRgb.description: The color of this shared drive as an RGB hex string.

orgUnitId.title: Org Unit Id
orgUnitId.description: The organizational unit of this shared drive. This field is only populated on drives.list responses when the useDomainAdminAccess parameter is set to true.

createdTime.title: Created Time
createdTime.description: The time at which the shared drive was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

capabilities.title: DriveCapabilities

canCopy.title: Can Copy
canCopy.description: Whether users with reader or commenter permissions can copy files in this shared drive.

canEdit.title: Can Edit
canEdit.description: Whether users with writer permissions can edit files in this shared drive.

canShare.title: Can Share
canShare.description: Whether the current user can share files or folders in this shared drive.

canRename.title: Can Rename
canRename.description: Whether users with writer permissions can rename files or folders in this shared drive.

canComment.title: Can Comment
canComment.description: Whether users with commenter permissions can comment on files in this shared drive.

canDownload.title: Can Download
canDownload.description: Whether users with reader or commenter permissions can download files in this shared drive.

canAddChildren.title: Can Add Children
canAddChildren.description: Whether users with writer permissions can add children to folders in this shared drive.

canDeleteDrive.title: Can Delete Drive
canDeleteDrive.description: Whether the current user can delete this shared drive. Attempting to delete the shared drive may still fail if there are untrashed items inside the shared drive.

canRenameDrive.title: Can Rename Drive
canRenameDrive.description: Whether the current user can rename this shared drive.

canListChildren.title: Can List Children
canListChildren.description: Whether users with reader or commenter permissions can list the children of folders in this shared drive.

canManageMembers.title: Can Manage Members
canManageMembers.description: Whether the current user can add members to this shared drive or remove them or change their role.

canReadRevisions.title: Can Read Revisions
canReadRevisions.description: Whether the current user can read the revisions resource of files in this shared drive.

canTrashChildren.title: Can Trash Children
canTrashChildren.description: Whether users with writer permissions can trash children from folders in this shared drive. This is deprecated and now identical to canDeleteDrive.

canDeleteChildren.title: Can Delete Children
canDeleteChildren.description: Whether users with writer permissions can delete children from folders in this shared drive. This is deprecated and now identical to canDeleteDrive.

canChangeDriveBackground.title: Can Change Drive Background
canChangeDriveBackground.description: Whether the current user can change the background of this shared drive.

canChangeDomainUsersOnlyRestriction.title: Can Change Domain Users Only Restriction
canChangeDomainUsersOnlyRestriction.description: Whether the current user can change the domainUsersOnly restriction of this shared drive.

canChangeDriveMembersOnlyRestriction.title: Can Change Drive Members Only Restriction
canChangeDriveMembersOnlyRestriction.description: Whether the current user can change the driveMembersOnly restriction of this shared drive.

canChangeCopyRequiresWriterPermissionRes.title: Can Change Copy Requires Writer Permission Restriction
canChangeCopyRequiresWriterPermissionRes.description: Whether the current user can change the copyRequiresWriterPermission restriction of this shared drive.

canChangeSharingFoldersRequiresOrganizer.title: Can Change Sharing Folders Requires Organizer Permission Restriction
canChangeSharingFoldersRequiresOrganizer.description: Whether the current user can change the sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermission restriction of this shared drive.

capabilities.description: Capabilities the current user has on this shared drive.

restrictions.title: DriveRestrictions

domainUsersOnly.title: Domain Users Only
domainUsersOnly.description: Whether access to this shared drive and items inside this shared drive is restricted to users of the domain to which this shared drive belongs. This restriction may be overridden by other sharing policies controlled outside of Drive.

driveMembersOnly.title: Drive Members Only
driveMembersOnly.description: Whether access to items inside this shared drive is restricted to its members.

adminManagedRestrictions.title: Admin Managed Restrictions
adminManagedRestrictions.description: Whether administrative privileges on this shared drive are required to modify restrictions.

copyRequiresWriterPermission.title: Copy Requires Writer Permission
copyRequiresWriterPermission.description: Whether the options to copy, print, or download files inside this shared drive, should be disabled for readers and commenters. When true, only permissions with write access will be able to copy, print, or download files.

sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermissio.title: Sharing Folders Requires Organizer Permission
sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermissio.description: Whether the ability to share folders in this shared drive is restricted to users with organizer_role for this shared drive.

restrictions.description: A set of restrictions that apply to this shared drive or items inside this shared drive.

backgroundImageLink.title: Background Image Link
backgroundImageLink.description: A short-lived link to this shared drive's background image.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new shared drive in Google Drive, allowing users to collaborate on files and organize content. Specify the name, ID, and settings for the drive, and ensure it meets the necessary criteria to avoid conflicts.

name: Create email draft
description: Creates a gmail email draft, supporting to/cc/bcc, subject, plain/html body (ensure `is html=true` for html), attachments, and threading.

body.title: Body
examples:
Hello Team,

Please find the attached report for your review.

Best regards,
Your Name

Meeting Confirmation

This email confirms our meeting scheduled for next Tuesday.
body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML); `is_html` must be True if HTML.

is_html.title: Is Html
is_html.description: Set to True if `body` is HTML, otherwise the action may fail.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

thread_id.title: Thread Id
thread_id.description: ID of an existing Gmail thread to reply to; omit for new thread.

attachment.title: FileUploadable
attachment.description: File to attach to the email.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Gmail API response with created draft details, including ID.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a draft email in Gmail. It allows specifying recipients, subject, body content, and attachments, enabling users to prepare emails for sending at a later time.

name: Create Event
description: Creates an event on a google calendar, needing rfc3339 utc start/end times (end after start) and write access to the calendar. by default, adds the organizer as an attendee unless exclude organizer is set to true.

location.title: Location
location.description: Geographic location of the event as free-form text.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone name (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Required if datetime is naive. If datetime includes timezone info (Z or offset), this field is optional and defaults to UTC.

eventType.title: Event Type
eventType.description: Type of the event, immutable post-creation. Supported types: 'birthday' (all-day with annual recurrence), 'default' (regular event), 'focusTime' (focus-time event), 'outOfOffice' (out-of-office event), 'workingLocation' (working location event). Note: 'fromGmail' events cannot be created via API.

recurrence.title: Recurrence
recurrence.description: List of RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE, EXDATE lines for recurring events. Supported frequencies are DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Target calendar: 'primary' for the user's main calendar, or the calendar's email address.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the event. Can contain HTML. Optional.

send_updates.title: Send Updates
send_updates.description: Defaults to True. Whether to send updates to the attendees.

start_datetime.title: Start Datetime
start_datetime.description: Naive date/time (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) with NO offsets or Z. e.g. '2025-01-16T13:00:00'

exclude_organizer.title: Exclude Organizer
exclude_organizer.description: If True, the organizer will NOT be added as an attendee. Default is False (organizer is included).

guests_can_modify.title: Guests Can Modify
guests_can_modify.description: If True, guests can modify the event.

create_meeting_room.title: Create Meeting Room
create_meeting_room.description: If true, a Google Meet link is created and added to the event. CRITICAL: As of 2024, this REQUIRES a paid Google Workspace account ($13+/month). Personal Gmail accounts will fail with 'Invalid conference type value' error. Solutions: 1) Upgrade to Workspace, 2) Use domain-wide delegation with Workspace user, 3) Use the new Google Meet REST API, or 4) Create events without conferences. See https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-nodejs-client/issues/3234

event_duration_hour.title: Event Duration Hour
event_duration_hour.description: Number of hours (0-24). Increase by 1 here rather than passing 60 in `event_duration_minutes`

guestsCanInviteOthers.title: Guests Can Invite Others
guestsCanInviteOthers.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event.

event_duration_minutes.title: Event Duration Minutes
event_duration_minutes.description: Duration in minutes (0-59 ONLY). NEVER use 60+ minutes - use event_duration_hour=1 instead. Maximum value is 59.

guestsCanSeeOtherGuests.title: Guests Can See Other Guests
guestsCanSeeOtherGuests.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are.

workingLocationProperties.title: Working Location Properties
workingLocationProperties.description: Working location properties for workingLocation events. Must include 'type' field with value: 'homeOffice', 'officeLocation', or 'customLocation'.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Full representation of the created event resource from Google Calendar API, including ID, summary, times, attendees, etc.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new event in a specified Google Calendar. By default, it attempts to add a Google Meet link to events (works for Workspace accounts, gracefully falls back for personal Gmail accounts). It allows users to set the event details and handle attendance options, including whether to include the organizer as an attendee. Use start_datetime with event_duration_hour and event_duration_minutes to specify the event time. The end_datetime parameter is not supported - use duration fields instead.

name.description: Output only. Resource name of the ExpandedDataSet.

createTime.description: Output only. Time when this ExpandedDataSet was created.

updateTime.title: Update Time

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional. Description of the expanded data set.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: Required. Human-readable display name for the expanded data set.

metricNames.title: Metric Names
metricNames.description: Required. List of metric names to include in the expanded data set.

dimensionNames.title: Dimension Names
dimensionNames.description: Required. List of dimension names to include in the expanded data set.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Create File or Folder
description: Creates a new file or folder with metadata. use to create empty files or folders, or files with content by providing it in the request body (though this action primarily focuses on metadata creation).

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: A comma-separated list of fields to include in the response.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the file.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#file'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The list of parent IDs for the file.

version.title: Version
version.description: A monotonically increasing version number for the file. This reflects every change made to the file on the server, even those not visible to the user.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows the user to create a new file or folder in a specified location. Users can provide metadata such as the name, MIME type, and parent folder IDs to specify the properties of the file being created.

name: Create a File from Text
description: Creates a new file in google drive from provided text content (up to 10mb), supporting various formats including automatic conversion to google workspace types.

file_name.title: File Name
file_name.description: Desired name for the new file on Google Drive.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type for the new file, determining how Google Drive interprets its content.

parent_id.title: Parent Id
parent_id.description: ID of the parent folder in Google Drive; if omitted, the file is created in the root of 'My Drive'.

text_content.title: Text Content
text_content.description: Plain text content to be written into the new file.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource type. For example, for a file, this is 'drive#file'.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

trashed.title: Trashed
trashed.description: Whether the file has been trashed, either explicitly or from a trashed parent folder.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive will attempt to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content if no value is provided. The value cannot be changed unless a new revision is uploaded.

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the file.

webViewLink.title: Web View Link
webViewLink.description: A link for opening the file in a relevant Google editor or viewer in a browser.

webContentLink.title: Web Content Link
webContentLink.description: A link for downloading the content of the file in a browser. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new file in Google Drive using the text content provided. It allows for various MIME types and can also create files in subfolders if specified. CRITICAL: The parent_id parameter requires a valid, existing Google Drive folder ID that you have access to - NOT a folder name. If you receive a 404 'File not found' error, verify the folder ID exists and you have access. Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FOLDER to get the folder ID from a folder name before calling this action.

name: Create a folder
description: Creates a new folder in google drive, optionally within a parent folder specified by its id or name; if a parent name is provided but not found, the action will fail.

parent_id.title: Parent Id
parent_id.description: ID or name of the parent folder. If a name is provided, the action attempts to find it. If an ID is provided, it must be a valid Google Drive folder ID. If omitted, the folder is created in the Drive root.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique identifier of the created folder.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#file'.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the created folder.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The ID(s) of the parent folder(s).

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the created folder, which will be 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new folder in Google Drive. You can specify a name for the folder and an optional parent folder by its ID or name. Important: The parent folder must already exist - this action will not create parent folders automatically.

name: Create Footer
description: Tool to create a new footer in a google document. use when you need to add a footer, optionally specifying its type and the section it applies to.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of footer to create.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document to create the footer in.

section_break_location.title: Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the beginning of the segment.

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

section_break_location.description: The location of the SectionBreak immediately preceding the section whose SectionStyle this footer should belong to. If this is unset or refers to the first section break in the document, the footer applies to the document style.

footer_id.title: Footer Id
footer_id.description: The ID of the created footer.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document the footer was created in.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows you to create a footer for a specified Google Document. You can define the type of footer and specify the section where it should be applied.

name: Create Footnote
description: Tool to create a new footnote in a google document. use this when you need to add a footnote at a specific location or at the end of the document body.

location.title: Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units. The index is relative to the beginning of the segment specified by segment_id.

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

location.description: Inserts the footnote reference at a specific index in the document. The segmentId within this Location object must be empty as footnotes can only be inserted in the document body.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document to create the footnote in.

endOfSegmentLocation.title: EndOfSegmentLocation

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

endOfSegmentLocation.description: Inserts the footnote reference at the end of the document body. The segmentId within this EndOfSegmentLocation object must be empty.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document where the footnote was created.

footnoteId.title: Footnote Id
footnoteId.description: The ID of the newly created footnote.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a footnote in a specified location within a Google Document. You can specify either the exact location within the document or choose to add it at the end of the document body.

tabId.title: Tab Id
