name: Create label
description: Creates a new label with a unique name in the specified user's gmail account.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: The email address of the user in whose account the label will be created.

label_name.title: Label Name
label_name.description: The name for the new label. Must be unique within the account, non-blank, maximum length 225 characters, cannot contain ',' or '/', not only whitespace, and must not be a reserved system label (e.g., INBOX, DRAFTS, SENT).

text_color.title: Text Color
text_color.description: The text color of the label, in hex (e.g., "#000000").

background_color.title: Background Color
background_color.description: The background color of the label, in hex (e.g., "#FFFFFF").

label_list_visibility.title: Label List Visibility
label_list_visibility.description: Controls how the label is displayed in the label list in the Gmail sidebar.

message_list_visibility.title: Message List Visibility
message_list_visibility.description: Controls how messages with this label are displayed in the message list.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Full API response from Gmail for the created label.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new label in the user's Gmail account with the specified properties. It ensures that the label name is unique and validates the color selections for the label.

name: Create a Meet
description: Creates a new google meet space, optionally configuring its access type and entry point access controls.

access_type.title: Access Type
access_type.description: Access control level for the meeting space.

entry_point_access.title: Entry Point Access
entry_point_access.description: Access control for meeting entry points.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: The full JSON response from the Google Meet API upon successful space creation.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Create Named Range
description: Tool to create a new named range in a google document. use this to assign a name to a specific part of the document for easier reference or programmatic manipulation.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name for the new named range. Must be 1-256 characters.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document where the named range will be created.

rangeEndIndex.title: Range End Index
rangeEndIndex.description: The zero-based end index of the range.

rangeSegmentId.title: Range Segment Id
rangeSegmentId.description: Optional. The ID of the header, footer, or footnote. Empty for document body.

rangeStartIndex.title: Range Start Index
rangeStartIndex.description: The zero-based start index of the range.

namedRangeId.title: Named Range Id
namedRangeId.description: The ID of the created named range.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a named range in a Google Document. A named range allows you to define a specific part of the document with a name for easier referencing and manipulation later on.

name: Create Paragraph Bullets
description: Tool to add bullets to paragraphs within a specified range in a google document. use when you need to format a list or a set of paragraphs as bullet points.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document to update.

range.title: Range

endIndex.title: End Index
endIndex.description: The zero-based end index of the range, exclusive.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer, or footnote that this range is contained in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

startIndex.title: Start Index
startIndex.description: The zero-based start index of the range, inclusive.

range.description: The range to apply the bullet preset to.

bulletPreset.title: Bullet Preset
bulletPreset.description: The kinds of bullet glyphs to be used.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: The replies to the request. For this action, it should contain one reply.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document that the bullets were added to.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action adds bullet points to specified paragraphs in a Google Document. It allows users to format lists within a defined range, enhancing document presentation.

name: Create Reply
description: Tool to create a reply to a comment in google drive. use when you need to respond to an existing comment on a file.

action.title: Action
action.description: The action the reply performed to the parent comment. Valid values are: resolve, reopen.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.

content.title: Content
content.description: The plain text content of the reply. HTML content is not supported.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#reply'.

action.title: Action
action.description: The action the reply performed to the parent comment. Valid values are: resolve, reopen.

author.title: Author

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#user'.

photo_link.title: Photo Link
photo_link.description: A short-lived link to the user's profile photo, if available.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: A short-lived display name for the user.

email_address.title: Email Address
email_address.description: The email address of the user. This is only populated if the drive.readonly.metadata scope is used.

permission_id.title: Permission Id
permission_id.description: The user's ID as visible in Permission resources.

author.description: The user who created the reply.

content.title: Content
content.description: The plain text content of the reply.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Whether the reply has been deleted. A deleted reply has no content.

created_time.title: Created Time
created_time.description: The time the reply was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

html_content.title: Html Content
html_content.description: The HTML content of the reply.

modified_time.title: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The last time the reply was modified (RFC 3339 date-time).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows users to create a reply to a comment on a specific file in Google Drive. It requires the file ID, comment ID, and the content of the reply.

name: Create Shortcut to File/Folder
description: Tool to create a shortcut to a file or folder in google drive. use when you need to link to an existing drive item from another location without duplicating it.

target_id.title: Target Id
target_id.description: The ID of the file or folder that this shortcut points to.

includeLabels.title: Include Labels
includeLabels.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

target_mime_type.title: Target Mime Type
target_mime_type.description: The MIME type of the target file or folder. While optional, providing it can be helpful.

supportsAllDrives.title: Supports All Drives
supportsAllDrives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Recommended to set to true if interacting with shared drives.

keepRevisionForever.title: Keep Revision Forever
keepRevisionForever.description: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision.

ignoreDefaultVisibility.title: Ignore Default Visibility
ignoreDefaultVisibility.description: Whether to ignore the domain's default visibility settings for the created file.

includePermissionsForView.title: Include Permissions For View
includePermissionsForView.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#file'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

teamDriveId.title: Team Drive Id
teamDriveId.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

shortcutDetails.title: ShortcutDetails

targetId.title: Target Id
targetId.description: The ID of the file or folder that this shortcut points to.

targetMimeType.title: Target Mime Type
targetMimeType.description: The MIME type of the target.

shortcutDetails.description: Shortcut file details. Only populated for shortcut files.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a shortcut to a specified file or folder in Google Drive. It allows users to access an existing Drive item from another location without making a copy of it.

name: Create spreadsheet column
description: Creates a new column in a google spreadsheet, requiring a valid `spreadsheet id` and an existing `sheet id`; an out-of-bounds `insert index` may append/prepend the column.

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The numeric identifier of the specific sheet (tab) within the spreadsheet where the column will be added.

insert_index.title: Insert Index
insert_index.description: The 0-based index at which the new column will be inserted. For example, an index of 0 inserts the column before the current first column (A), and an index of 1 inserts it between the current columns A and B.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet where the column will be created.

inherit_from_before.title: Inherit From Before
inherit_from_before.description: If true, the new column inherits properties (e.g., formatting, width) from the column immediately to its left (the preceding column). If false (default), it inherits from the column immediately to its right (the succeeding column). This is ignored if there is no respective preceding or succeeding column.

spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: A dictionary object representing the updated spreadsheet resource. This object contains details about the spreadsheet's properties and its sheets. Note: Grid data (cell values) is not included in this response.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new column in the specified Google Spreadsheet. You can specify the target sheet by its numeric ID (sheet_id) or by name (sheet_name). If neither is provided, the column is added to the first sheet.

name: Create spreadsheet row
description: Inserts a new, empty row into a specified sheet of a google spreadsheet at a given index, optionally inheriting formatting from the row above.

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The numeric identifier of the sheet (tab) within the spreadsheet where the row will be inserted. This ID (gid) is found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., '0' for the first sheet).

insert_index.title: Insert Index
insert_index.description: The 0-based index at which the new row should be inserted. For example, an index of 0 inserts the row at the beginning of the sheet. If the index is greater than the current number of rows, the row is appended.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet. This ID is found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., '1qpyC0XzHc_-_d824s2VfopkHh7D0jW4aXCS1D_AlGA').

inherit_from_before.title: Inherit From Before
inherit_from_before.description: If True, the newly inserted row will inherit formatting and properties from the row immediately preceding its insertion point. If False, it will have default formatting.

spreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: A dictionary representing the updated Google Spreadsheet resource, including details about the newly inserted row if 'includeSpreadsheetInResponse' was true in the underlying API call (which it is by default in this action).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new row in the specified Google Spreadsheet at the given index. It can also inherit formatting from the preceding row if requested.

name: Create a task list
description: Creates a new task list with the specified title.

tasklist_title.title: Tasklist Title
tasklist_title.description: Title for the new task list. The maximum allowed length is 1024 characters.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource. This is always 'tasks#taskList'.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task list. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Last modification time of the task list (as a RFC 3339 timestamp).

selfLink.title: Self Link
selfLink.description: URL pointing to this task list. Used to retrieve, update, or delete this task list.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Delete Comment
description: Deletes a comment from a file. use when you need to remove a specific comment from a google drive file.

message.title: Message
message.description: A message indicating the success of the operation.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action allows you to delete a specific comment from a designated file on Google Drive. 
    It requires the file ID and comment ID to identify which comment to remove.

name: Delete Content Range in Document
description: Tool to delete a range of content from a google document. use when you need to remove a specific portion of text or other structural elements within a document.

range.title: Range

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The ID of the tab the range is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab. In a document with a single tab, if provided, it must match the singular tab's ID; if omitted, it applies to the singular tab. In a multi-tab document, if provided, it applies to the specified tab; if omitted, it applies to the first tab.

endIndex.title: End Index
endIndex.description: The zero-based end index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the beginning of the segment.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer, or footnote the range is in. An empty segment ID or omitting this field signifies the document's body.

startIndex.title: Start Index
startIndex.description: The zero-based start index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the beginning of the segment.

range.description: The range of content to delete. Deleting text across paragraph boundaries may merge paragraphs and affect styles. Certain deletions can invalidate document structure (e.g., part of a surrogate pair, last newline of critical elements, incomplete deletion of tables/equations).

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document from which to delete content. This ID can be found in the URL of the Google Doc.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies from the batchUpdate request. For DeleteContentRange, this is typically empty or contains information about the operation's success.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document that the content was deleted from.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specified range of content from a Google Document. It is useful for removing text or structural components that are no longer needed. Important: The endIndex must not include the final newline at the end of a segment, as this newline cannot be deleted.

name: Delete Dimension (Rows/Columns)
description: Tool to delete specified rows or columns from a sheet in a google spreadsheet. use when you need to remove a range of rows or columns.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet.

response_ranges.title: Response Ranges
response_ranges.description: Limits the ranges of cells included in the response spreadsheet.

delete_dimension_request.title: Delete Dimension Request

range.title: Range

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet from which to delete the dimension.

end_index.title: End Index
end_index.description: The zero-based end index of the range to delete, exclusive. The end index must be greater than the start index.

start_index.title: Start Index
start_index.description: The zero-based start index of the range to delete, inclusive. The start index must be less than the end index.

range.description: The range of the dimension to delete.

delete_dimension_request.description: The details for the delete dimension request object.

response_include_grid_data.title: Response Include Grid Data
response_include_grid_data.description: True if grid data should be returned. This parameter is ignored if a field mask was set in the request.

include_spreadsheet_in_response.title: Include Spreadsheet In Response
include_spreadsheet_in_response.description: Determines if the update response should include the spreadsheet resource.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies to the batch update requests. For DeleteDimensionRequest, the reply is an empty object if successful.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet that was updated.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Deletes rows or columns from a Google Sheet. Specify the range of rows or columns you want to remove, and they will be permanently deleted from the spreadsheet.

name: Delete Draft
description: Permanently deletes a specific gmail draft using its id; ensure the draft exists and the user has necessary permissions for the given `user id`.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user; 'me' is recommended.

draft_id.title: Draft Id
draft_id.description: Immutable ID of the draft to delete, typically obtained when the draft was created.

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates successful deletion; an exception is raised on error rather than returning false.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specified Gmail draft by its ID. It requires the user's permissions to perform this operation on the specified draft.

name: Delete Shared Drive
description: Tool to permanently delete a shared drive. use when you need to remove a shared drive and its contents (if specified).

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive.

allowItemDeletion.title: Allow Item Deletion
allowItemDeletion.description: Whether any items inside the shared drive should also be deleted. This option is only supported when `useDomainAdminAccess` is also set to `true`.

useDomainAdminAccess.title: Use Domain Admin Access
useDomainAdminAccess.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows for the permanent deletion of a specified shared drive. It can optionally delete all items within the drive if the appropriate permissions are granted.

name: Delete event
description: Deletes a specified event by `event id` from a google calendar (`calendar id`); this action is idempotent and raises a 404 error if the event is not found.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Unique identifier of the event to delete, typically obtained upon event creation.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the Google Calendar (e.g., email address, specific ID, or 'primary' for the authenticated user's main calendar) from which the event will be deleted.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Details the deletion outcome: `{'status': 'success'}` for a standard 204 No Content, or `{'message': <API_JSON_response>}` if the API returns a body on other success statuses.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Deletes an event from a specified Google Calendar using its unique event ID. This action ensures that if the same request is made multiple times, the result will remain consistent.

name: Delete Footer
description: Tool to delete a footer from a google document. use when you need to remove a footer from a specific section or the default footer.

tab_id.title: Tab Id
tab_id.description: The tab that contains the footer to delete. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

footer_id.title: Footer Id
footer_id.description: The ID of the footer to delete. If this footer is defined on DocumentStyle, the reference to this footer is removed, resulting in no footer of that type for the first section of the document. If this footer is defined on a SectionStyle, the reference to this footer is removed and the footer of that type is now continued from the previous section.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document to delete the footer from.

reply.title: Reply
reply.description: The response from the Google Docs API. Can be a dict or list of dicts.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document from which the footer was deleted.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specific footer from a document. It allows you to remove footers either from the entire document or from specified sections as needed.

name: Delete Header
description: Deletes the header from the specified section or the default header if no section is specified. use this tool to remove a header from a google document.

tab_id.title: Tab Id
tab_id.description: The tab containing the header to delete. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

header_id.title: Header Id
header_id.description: The ID of the header to delete. If this header is defined on `DocumentStyle`, the reference to this header is removed, resulting in no header of that type for the first section of the document. If this header is defined on a `SectionStyle`, the reference to this header is removed and the header of that type is now continued from the previous section.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document from which to delete the header.

reply.title: Reply
reply.description: Reply from Google Docs API after deleting the header.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document from which the header was deleted.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Deletes the header from the specified section or the default header if no section is specified. Use this action to remove a header from a Google Document.

name: Delete message
description: Permanently deletes a specific email message by its id from a gmail mailbox; for `user id`, use 'me' for the authenticated user or an email address to which the authenticated user has delegated access.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address. The special value 'me' refers to the authenticated user.

message_id.title: Message Id
message_id.description: Identifier of the email message to delete.

success.title: Success
success.description: True if the message was successfully deleted.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specified email message from a Gmail mailbox. You can use it to remove messages either for the authenticated user or another user with delegated access.

name: Delete Named Range
description: Tool to delete a named range from a google document. use when you need to remove a previously defined named range by its id or name.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document.

deleteNamedRange.title: Delete Named Range

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the range(s) to delete. All named ranges with the given name will be deleted.

namedRangeId.title: Named Range Id
namedRangeId.description: The ID of the named range to delete.

tabsCriteria.title: TabsCriteria

tabIds.title: Tab Ids
tabIds.description: The list of tab IDs in which the request executes.

tabsCriteria.description: Optional. The criteria used to specify which tab(s) the range deletion should occur in. When omitted, the range deletion is applied to all tabs.

deleteNamedRange.description: Specifies the named range to delete.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies. For a successful DeleteNamedRange, this might be an empty object or have minimal info.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document to which the changes were applied.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows for the deletion of a specified named range in a Google Document. Users can delete a named range by providing either its ID or its name, streamlining document management.

name: Delete Paragraph Bullets
description: Tool to remove bullets from paragraphs within a specified range in a google document. use when you need to clear bullet formatting from a section of a document.

range.title: Range

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The ID of the tab the range is in. If omitted, it applies to the first tab or the singular tab in a single-tab document.

endIndex.title: End Index
endIndex.description: The zero-based end index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the start of the segment.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer, or footnote the range is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

startIndex.title: Start Index
startIndex.description: The zero-based start index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the start of the segment.

range.description: The range of the document from which to delete paragraph bullets. The range is applied to the document body by default. To specify a different segment (e.g. header, footer), include segment_id in the range object.

tab_id.title: Tab Id
tab_id.description: The ID of the tab the range is in. If omitted, it applies to the first tab or the singular tab in a single-tab document.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document to update.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies from the Google Docs API, one for each request in the batch. For DeleteParagraphBullets, this will typically be an empty object if successful.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document that the bullets were deleted from.

write_control.title: Write Control
write_control.description: Provides control over how write requests are executed.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action removes bullet points from the specified range of paragraphs in a Google Document. It is particularly useful for formatting adjustments in document editing.

name: Delete Permission
description: Deletes a permission from a file by permission id. use when you need to revoke access for a specific user or group from a file.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: The ID of the file or shared drive.

supportsAllDrives.title: Supports All Drives
supportsAllDrives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

useDomainAdminAccess.title: Use Domain Admin Access
useDomainAdminAccess.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates whether the permission was deleted successfully.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Deletes a specific permission from a file using the permission ID. IMPORTANT: You must first use the 'List Permissions' action to get the permission IDs for a file. Permission IDs are opaque identifiers assigned by Google's API and cannot be guessed.

name: Delete Reply
description: Tool to delete a specific reply by reply id. use when you need to remove a reply from a comment on a file.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Delete Sheet
description: Tool to delete a sheet (worksheet) from a spreadsheet. use when you need to remove a specific sheet from a google sheet document.

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet to delete. If the sheet is of DATA_SOURCE type, the associated DataSource is also deleted.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet from which to delete the sheet.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies for each request in the batch. For a DeleteSheetRequest, the reply will be an empty object {} upon success.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specified sheet from a given spreadsheet. It ensures that the selected sheet, along with its associated data sources if applicable, is removed from the document.

name: Delete Table
description: Tool to delete an entire table from a google document. use when you have the document id and the specific start and end index of the table element to be removed. the table's range can be found by inspecting the document's content structure.

tab_id.title: Tab Id
tab_id.description: The ID of the tab containing the table, if the document uses tabs. If omitted, the action applies to the first tab or the only tab.

segment_id.title: Segment Id
segment_id.description: The ID of the segment (e.g., header, footer) containing the table. If omitted or empty, the table is assumed to be in the main document body.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the Google Document from which the table will be deleted.

table_end_index.title: Table End Index
table_end_index.description: The end index (UTF-16 code unit) of the table's range to be deleted.

table_start_index.title: Table Start Index
table_start_index.description: The start index (UTF-16 code unit) of the table's range to be deleted.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies corresponding to each request in the batch update. For a successful deleteContentRange, the reply is typically empty.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document where the table was deleted.

writeControl.title: Write Control
writeControl.description: The write control that was applied to the request.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action deletes a specified table from a Google Document using its document ID and the range of UTF-16 indices that define the table's location. This allows users to manage document content effectively by removing unwanted tables.

name: Delete Table Column
description: Tool to delete a column from a table in a google document. use this tool when you need to remove a specific column from an existing table within a document.

tableCellLocation.title: Table Cell Location

rowIndex.title: Row Index
rowIndex.description: The zero-based row index. For example, the second row in the table has a row index of 1.

columnIndex.title: Column Index
columnIndex.description: The zero-based column index. For example, the second column in the table has a column index of 1.

tableStartLocation.title: Table Start Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units.

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

tableStartLocation.description: The location where the table starts in the document.

tableCellLocation.description: The reference table cell location from which the column will be deleted.

requests.title: Requests
requests.description: A list of requests to be applied to the document.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document to delete the table column from.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: The replies to the requests. This field is empty if the requests were successful.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows users to delete a specific column from a table in a Google Document. It takes the document ID and details about the column location to perform the deletion.

columnIndex.title: Column Index

tableStartLocation.title: Table Start Location

index.title: Index

tabId.title: Tab Id

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

tableStartLocation.description: The location where the table starts in the document.

tableCellLocation.description: The reference table cell location from which the row will be deleted.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: Raw list of replies from the batchUpdate request.

documentId.title: Document Id

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Deletes a specified row in a table within a Google Document. It allows for maintaining document accuracy by removing unnecessary or irrelevant data in tabular format.

name: Distance Matrix
description: Calculates travel distance and time for a matrix of origins and destinations. supports different modes of transportation and options like departure/arrival times. use when needing to determine travel metrics between multiple points.

key.title: Key
key.description: Your application's API key. This key identifies your application for purposes of quota management.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Specifies the mode of transport to use.

avoid.title: Avoid
avoid.description: Indicates that the calculated route should avoid the specified features. Multiple values can be pipe-separated e.g. 'tolls|highways'.

units.title: Units
units.description: Specifies the unit system to use when displaying results. The default is metric.

region.title: Region
region.description: The region code, specified as a ccTLD ('top-level domain') two-character value. This helps influence results based on the region.

origins.title: Origins
origins.description: The starting point for calculating travel distance and time. You can supply one or more locations separated by the pipe character (|), in the form of a place ID (prefixed with place_id:), an address, latitude/longitude coordinates (e.g., '40.7128,-74.0060'), a plus code, or an encoded polyline (prefixed with enc: and a colon).

language.title: Language
language.description: The language in which to return results. See the list of supported languages: https://developers.google.com/maps/faq#languagesupport

arrival_time.title: Arrival Time
arrival_time.description: Specifies the desired time of arrival for transit directions, in seconds since midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC. You can specify either departure_time or arrival_time, but not both.

destinations.title: Destinations
destinations.description: One or more locations to use as the finishing point for calculating travel distance and time. Accepts the same formats as origins.

transit_mode.title: Transit Mode
transit_mode.description: Specifies one or more preferred modes of transit. This parameter may only be specified for transit directions. Multiple values can be pipe-separated e.g. 'bus|train'.

traffic_model.title: Traffic Model
traffic_model.description: Specifies the assumptions to use when calculating time in traffic. This parameter is only used if the request includes a departure_time and mode is 'driving'.

departure_time.title: Departure Time
departure_time.description: Specifies the desired time of departure. You can specify the time as an integer in seconds since midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC, or as the string 'now'. Required for duration_in_traffic.

transit_routing_preference.title: Transit Routing Preference
transit_routing_preference.description: Specifies preferences for transit routes. This parameter may only be specified for transit directions.

fare.title: Fare

text.title: Text
text.description: The total fare amount, formatted in the requested language.

value.title: Value
value.description: The total fare amount, in the currency specified.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: An ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency that the amount is expressed in.

fare.description: If present, contains the total fare (that is, the total ticket costs) on this route.

status.title: Status
status.description: A status for the element. See DistanceMatrixElementStatus for more information.

distance.title: TextValueObject
distance.description: The total distance of this route, expressed in meters (value) and as text.

duration.title: TextValueObject
duration.description: The length of time it takes to travel this route, expressed in seconds (the value field) and as text.

duration_in_traffic.title: TextValueObject
duration_in_traffic.description: The length of time it takes to travel this route, based on current and historical traffic conditions.

elements.title: Elements
elements.description: An array of elements, which in turn each contain a status, duration, and distance element.

rows.title: Rows
rows.description: An array of rows, each row containing one origin paired with each destination.

status.title: Status
status.description: Contains the status of the request (e.g., OK, INVALID_REQUEST).

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: A string containing the human-readable text of any errors encountered while the request was being processed.

origin_addresses.title: Origin Addresses
origin_addresses.description: An array of addresses as returned by the API from your original request for origins.

destination_addresses.title: Destination Addresses
destination_addresses.description: An array of addresses as returned by the API from your original request for destinations.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Download a file from Google Drive
description: Downloads a file from google drive by its id. for google workspace documents (docs, sheets, slides), optionally exports to a specified `mime type`. for other file types, downloads in their native format regardless of mime type.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: The unique identifier of the file to be downloaded from Google Drive. This ID can typically be found in the file's URL in Google Drive or obtained from API calls that list files.

mime_type.title: MimeType
mime_type.description: Target MIME type for exporting Google Workspace documents (e.g., Google Doc, Sheet, Slide). Supported export formats vary by file type; e.g., text/plain is only supported for Google Docs, not Sheets or Slides. This parameter is automatically ignored for non-Google Workspace files, which are downloaded in their native format. Only specify this when you want to export a Google Workspace document to a different format (e.g., export Google Doc to PDF).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#file".

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

downloaded_file_content.title: Downloaded File Content
downloaded_file_content.description: A reference to the downloaded file content. If the original file was a Google Workspace document and a mime_type was specified for export, this content will be in the exported format. Otherwise, it will be the original file content.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows users to download files from Google Drive using the file's unique identifier. It supports exporting Google Workspace files to different formats if specified.

name: Get Event Instances
description: Returns instances of the specified recurring event.

timeMax.title: Time Max
timeMax.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by start time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset.

timeMin.title: Time Min
timeMin.description: Lower bound (inclusive) for an event's end time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by end time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset.

timeZone.title: Time Zone
timeZone.description: Time zone used in the response. Optional. The default is the time zone of the calendar.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

calendarId.title: Calendar Id
calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the `calendarList.list` method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

maxResults.title: Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of events returned on one result page. By default the value is 250 events. The page size can never be larger than 2500 events. Optional.

showDeleted.title: Show Deleted
showDeleted.description: Whether to include deleted events (with status equals "cancelled") in the result. Cancelled instances of recurring events will still be included if `singleEvents` is False. Optional. The default is False.

maxAttendees.title: Max Attendees
maxAttendees.description: The maximum number of attendees to include in the response. If there are more than the specified number of attendees, only the participant is returned. Optional.

originalStart.title: Original Start
originalStart.description: The original start time of the instance in the result. Optional.

nextPageToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result.

nextSyncToken.title: Next Sync Token
nextSyncToken.description: Token used at a later point in time to retrieve only the entries that have changed since this result was returned.

method.title: Method
method.description: The method used by this reminder. Possible values are: 'email', 'popup'.

minutes.title: Minutes
minutes.description: Number of minutes before the start of the event when the reminder should trigger.

defaultReminders.title: Default Reminders
defaultReminders.description: The default reminders on the calendar for the authenticated user.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Returns individual occurrences (instances) of a SPECIFIC recurring event. You MUST provide the recurring event's ID (eventId) - use GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST action first to find recurring events and get their IDs. This is NOT for searching or listing all events in a time range; for that, use GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST instead.

name: List Events
description: Returns events on the specified calendar.

q.title: Q
q.description: Free text search terms to find events that match these terms in various fields. Optional.

iCalUID.title: I Cal Uid
iCalUID.description: Specifies an event ID in the iCalendar format to be provided in the response. Optional. Use this if you want to search for an event by its iCalendar ID.

orderBy.title: Order By
orderBy.description: The order of the events returned in the result. Optional. The default is an unspecified, stable order. Acceptable values are: "startTime", "updated".

timeMax.title: Time Max
timeMax.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by start time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset, for example, 2011-06-03T10:00:00-07:00, 2011-06-03T10:00:00Z. Milliseconds may be provided but are ignored. If timeMin is set, timeMax must be greater than timeMin.

timeMin.title: Time Min
timeMin.description: Lower bound (exclusive) for an event's end time to filter by. Optional. The default is not to filter by end time. Must be an RFC3339 timestamp with mandatory time zone offset, for example, 2011-06-03T10:00:00-07:00, 2011-06-03T10:00:00Z. Milliseconds may be provided but are ignored. If timeMax is set, timeMin must be smaller than timeMax.

timeZone.title: Time Zone
timeZone.description: Time zone used in the response. Optional. The default is the user's primary time zone.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

syncToken.title: Sync Token
syncToken.description: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of results from the previous list request. Optional. The default is to return all entries.

calendarId.title: Calendar Id
calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

eventTypes.title: Event Types
eventTypes.description: Event types to return. Optional. This parameter can be repeated multiple times to return events of different types. If unset, returns all event types. Acceptable values are: "birthday", "default", "focusTime", "fromGmail", "outOfOffice", "workingLocation".

maxResults.title: Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of events returned on one result page. The number of events in the resulting page may be less than this value, or none at all, even if there are more events matching the query. Incomplete pages can be detected by a non-empty nextPageToken field in the response. By default the value is 250 events. The page size can never be larger than 2500 events. Optional.

updatedMin.title: Updated Min
updatedMin.description: Lower bound for an event's last modification time (as a RFC3339 timestamp) to filter by. When specified, entries deleted since this time will always be included regardless of showDeleted. Optional. The default is not to filter by last modification time.

showDeleted.title: Show Deleted
showDeleted.description: Whether to include deleted events (with status equals "cancelled") in the result. Optional. The default is False.

maxAttendees.title: Max Attendees
maxAttendees.description: The maximum number of attendees to include in the response. If there are more than the specified number of attendees, only the participant is returned. Optional.

singleEvents.title: Single Events
singleEvents.description: Whether to expand recurring events into instances and only return single one-off events and instances of recurring events. Optional. The default is False.

showHiddenInvitations.title: Show Hidden Invitations
showHiddenInvitations.description: Whether to include hidden invitations in the result. Optional. The default is False.

sharedExtendedProperty.title: Shared Extended Property
sharedExtendedProperty.description: Extended properties constraint specified as propertyName=value. Matches only shared properties. This parameter might be repeated multiple times to return events that match all given constraints.

privateExtendedProperty.title: Private Extended Property
privateExtendedProperty.description: Extended properties constraint specified as propertyName=value. Matches only private properties. This parameter might be repeated multiple times to return events that match all given constraints.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource ("calendar#events").

items.title: Items
items.description: List of events on the calendar.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Last modification time of the calendar (as a RFC3339 timestamp). Read-only.

timeZone.title: Time Zone
timeZone.description: The time zone of the calendar. Read-only.

accessRole.title: Access Role
accessRole.description:
The user's access role for this calendar. Read-only. Possible values are: 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role and additionally allows any user with this role to modify and revoke access to the calendar.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the calendar. Read-only.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result. Omitted if no further results are available, in which case nextSyncToken is provided.

nextSyncToken.title: Next Sync Token
nextSyncToken.description: Token used at a later point in time to retrieve only the entries that have changed since this result was returned. Omitted if further results are available, in which case nextPageToken is provided.

defaultReminders.title: Default Reminders
defaultReminders.description: The default reminders on the calendar for the authenticated user. These reminders apply to all events on this calendar that do not explicitly override them (i.e. do not have reminders.useDefault set to True).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves events from a specified calendar. It allows for various filtering options such as event type, time range, and more to customize the results. To include hidden invitations that you have not yet responded to, set showHiddenInvitations=true. In the response, hidden invitations can be identified by an attendee entry with attendees[].self==true and attendees[].responseStatus=="needsAction". TIMEZONE NOTE: If results are unexpectedly empty when filtering by time, check that your timeMin/timeMax timezone offsets match the calendar's timezone. UTC times may not align with local calendar dates.

name: Move Event
description: Moves an event to another calendar, i.e., changes an event's organizer.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Event identifier. To retrieve event identifiers call the events.list method.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Calendar identifier of the source calendar. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: Calendar identifier of the destination calendar. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

send_updates.title: Send Updates
send_updates.description: Guests who should receive notifications about the change of the event's organizer. Acceptable values are: "all": Notifications are sent to all guests. "externalOnly": Notifications are sent to non-Google Calendar guests only. "none": No notifications are sent. This is the default value if left unspecified.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Moves an event from one calendar to another by changing its organizer. This action is useful for managing event assignments across different calendars.

name: Watch Events
description: Watch for changes to events resources.

id.title: Id
id.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel.

token.title: Token
token.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

params.title: EventsWatchRequestParams

ttl.title: Ttl
ttl.description: Time To Live for the notification channel.

params.description: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior. Optional.

address.title: Address
address.description: The address where notifications are delivered for this channel.

payload.title: Payload
payload.description: A Boolean value to indicate whether payload is wanted. Optional.

calendarId.title: Calendar Id
calendarId.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

id.title: Id
id.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies this as a notification channel. Value: "api#channel".

token.title: Token
token.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

expiration.title: Expiration
expiration.description: Date and time of notification channel expiration, expressed as a Unix timestamp, in milliseconds. Optional.

resourceId.title: Resource Id
resourceId.description: An opaque ID that identifies the resource being watched on this channel. Stable across different API versions.

resourceUri.title: Resource Uri
resourceUri.description: A version-specific identifier for the watched resource.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action sets up a notification channel for real-time updates on events in a specified calendar. Users can receive alerts whenever there are changes to the events, ensuring they stay informed.

name: Execute SQL on Spreadsheet
description: Execute sql queries against google sheets tables. supports select, insert, update, and delete operations with familiar sql syntax. tables are automatically detected and mapped from the spreadsheet structure.

sql.title: Sql
examples: UPDATE "Inventory" SET quantity = quantity - 10 WHERE sku = 'ABC123'
sql.description: SQL query to execute. Supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations.

dry_run.title: Dry Run
dry_run.description: Preview changes without applying them (for write operations)

delete_method.title: Delete Method
delete_method.description: For DELETE operations: 'clear' preserves row structure, 'remove_rows' shifts data up

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: Google Sheets ID

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: SQL operation performed: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

affected_rows.title: Affected Rows
affected_rows.description: Number of rows affected by the operation

human_description: This action executes SQL queries directly on Google Sheets tables, allowing users to perform SQL operations like SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and complex queries using WITH clauses (Common Table Expressions). It simplifies data manipulation within spreadsheets using familiar SQL syntax.

name: Fetch emails
description: Fetches a list of email messages from a gmail account, supporting filtering, pagination, and optional full content retrieval.

query.title: Query
query.description: Gmail advanced search query (e.g., 'from:user subject:meeting'). Supports operators like 'from:', 'to:', 'subject:', 'label:', 'has:attachment', 'is:unread', 'after:YYYY/MM/DD', 'before:YYYY/MM/DD', AND/OR/NOT. Use quotes for exact phrases. Omit for no query filter.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

verbose.title: Verbose
verbose.description: If false, uses optimized concurrent metadata fetching for faster performance (~75% improvement). If true, uses standard detailed message fetching. When false, only essential fields (subject, sender, recipient, time, labels) are guaranteed.

ids_only.title: Ids Only
ids_only.description: If true, only returns message IDs from the list API without fetching individual message details. Fastest option for getting just message IDs and thread IDs.

label_ids.title: Label Ids
label_ids.description: Filter by label IDs; only messages with all specified labels are returned. Common IDs: 'INBOX', 'SPAM', 'TRASH', 'UNREAD', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'CATEGORY_PRIMARY' (alias 'CATEGORY_PERSONAL'), 'CATEGORY_SOCIAL', 'CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS', 'CATEGORY_UPDATES', 'CATEGORY_FORUMS'. Use 'listLabels' action for custom IDs.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Token for retrieving a specific page, obtained from a previous response's `nextPageToken`. Omit for the first page.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of messages to retrieve per page.

include_payload.title: Include Payload
include_payload.description: Set to true to include full message payload (headers, body, attachments); false for metadata only.

include_spam_trash.title: Include Spam Trash
include_spam_trash.description: Set to true to include messages from 'SPAM' and 'TRASH'.

items.title: MessageBody

labelIds.title: Label Ids

threadId.title: Thread Id

messageId.title: Message Id
messageId.description: The message ID of the message

messages.description: List of retrieved email messages. Includes full content if `include_payload` was true, otherwise metadata.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page of results; use in subsequent `page_token` request. Empty if no more results.

resultSizeEstimate.title: Result Size Estimate
resultSizeEstimate.description: Estimated total messages matching the query (not just this page).

error.title: Error

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Fetch message by message ID
description: Fetches a specific email message by its id, provided the `message id` exists and is accessible to the authenticated `user id`.

format.title: Format

messageId.description: The message ID of the message

error.title: Error

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

description: Retrieves messages from a gmail thread using its `thread id`, where the thread must be accessible by the specified `user id`.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Opaque page token for fetching a specific page of messages if results are paginated.

threadId.title: Thread Id

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves messages from a specific Gmail thread. It requires the thread's ID and the user's ID to access the messages within that thread.

name: Modify File Labels
description: Modifies the set of labels applied to a file. returns a list of the labels that were added or modified. use when you need to programmatically change labels on a google drive file, such as adding, updating, or removing them.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: This is always drive#modifyLabelsRequest.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: This is always drive#labelModification.

label_id.title: Label Id
label_id.description: The ID of the label to modify.

remove_label.title: Remove Label
remove_label.description: If true, the label will be removed from the file.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: This is always drive#labelFieldModification.

field_id.title: Field Id
field_id.description: The ID of the field to be modified.

unset_values.title: Unset Values
unset_values.description: Unsets the values for this field.

set_date_values.title: Set Date Values
set_date_values.description: Replaces the value of a `date` field with these new values. The string must be in the RFC 3339 full-date format: YYYY-MM-DD.

set_text_values.title: Set Text Values
set_text_values.description: Sets the value of a `text` field.

set_user_values.title: Set User Values
set_user_values.description: Replaces a `user` field with these new values. The values must be valid email addresses.

set_integer_values.title: Set Integer Values
set_integer_values.description: Replaces the value of an `integer` field with these new values.

set_selection_values.title: Set Selection Values
set_selection_values.description: Replaces a `selection` field with these new values.

field_modifications.title: Field Modifications
field_modifications.description: The list of modifications to this label's fields.

label_modifications.title: Label Modifications
label_modifications.description: The list of modifications to apply to the labels on the file.

kind.description: This is always drive#modifyLabelsResponse.

kind.description: Identifies the resource type, this is always drive#label

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: A map of field IDs to field values.

revision_id.title: Revision Id
revision_id.description: The revision ID of the label.

modified_labels.title: Modified Labels
modified_labels.description: The list of labels which were added or updated by the request.

error.title: Error

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action modifies the labels assigned to a specific file in Google Drive. It allows users to add, update, or remove labels associated with the file.

name: Find event
description: Finds events in a specified google calendar using text query, time ranges (event start/end, last modification), and event types; ensure `timemin` is not chronologically after `timemax` if both are provided.

query.title: Query
query.description: Free-text search terms to find events. This query is matched against various event fields including summary, description, location, attendees' details (displayName, email), and organizer's details.

timeMax.title: Time Max
timeMax.description:
Upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time to filter by. Only events starting before this time are included. Accepts multiple formats:
1. RFC3339 timestamp (e.g., '2024-12-06T13:00:00Z')
2. Comma-separated date/time parts (e.g., '2024,12,06,13,00,00')
3. Simple datetime string (e.g., '2024-12-06 13:00:00')

timeMin.title: Time Min
timeMin.description:
Lower bound (exclusive) for an event's end time to filter by. Only events ending after this time are included. Accepts multiple formats:
1. RFC3339 timestamp (e.g., '2024-12-06T13:00:00Z')
2. Comma-separated date/time parts (e.g., '2024,12,06,13,00,00')
3. Simple datetime string (e.g., '2024-12-06 13:00:00')

order_by.title: Order By
order_by.description: Order of events: 'startTime' (ascending by start time) or 'updated' (ascending by last modification time).

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Token from a previous response's `nextPageToken` to fetch the subsequent page of results.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the Google Calendar to query. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the authenticated user, an email address for a specific user's calendar, or a calendar ID for other calendars.

event_types.title: Event Types
event_types.description: Event types to include: 'default' (regular event), 'focusTime' (focused work time), 'outOfOffice' (out-of-office time).

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of events per page (1-2500).

updated_min.title: Updated Min
updated_min.description:
Lower bound (exclusive) for an event's last modification time to filter by. Only events updated after this time are included. When specified, events deleted since this time are also included, regardless of the `show_deleted` parameter. Accepts multiple formats:
1. RFC3339 timestamp (e.g., '2024-12-06T13:00:00Z')
2. Comma-separated date/time parts (e.g., '2024,12,06,13,00,00')
3. Simple datetime string (e.g., '2024-12-06 13:00:00')

show_deleted.title: Show Deleted
show_deleted.description: Include deleted events (status 'cancelled') in the result.

single_events.title: Single Events
single_events.description: Expand recurring events into individual instances. If false, returns master recurring events.

event_data.title: Event Data
event_data.description: A dictionary containing the search results. Keys include: 'event_data' (a list of the found event objects matching Google Calendar API's event resource schema), 'nextPageToken' (string token for fetching the next page of results, null if no more pages), 'nextSyncToken' (string token for future incremental synchronization, may not always be present), and 'note' (an optional string with contextual information about the search).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves events from a Google Calendar based on specified search criteria including time ranges and event types. It allows users to find events that match certain search terms and can include deleted events if specified.

name: Find file
description: Tool to list or search for files and folders in google drive. use when you need to find specific files based on query criteria or list contents of a drive/folder.

q.title: Q
q.description: A query for filtering the file results. See Google Drive API documentation for query syntax. Example: \"name contains 'report' and mimeType = 'application/pdf'\"

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use '*' for all fields or a comma-separated list, e.g., 'nextPageToken,files(id,name,mimeType)'.

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: A comma-separated list of spaces to query. Supported values are 'drive', 'appDataFolder' and 'photos'.

corpora.title: Corpora
corpora.description: Corpora to query. 'user' for user's personal files, 'drive' for files in a specific shared drive (requires 'driveId'), 'domain' for files shared with the domain, 'allDrives' for all drives the user has access to.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: ID of the shared drive to search. Required if 'corpora' is 'drive'.

orderBy.title: Order By
orderBy.description: A comma-separated list of sort keys. Valid keys are 'createdTime', 'folder', 'modifiedByMeTime', 'modifiedTime', 'name', 'name_natural', 'quotaBytesUsed', 'recency', 'sharedWithMeTime', 'starred', and 'viewedByMeTime'. Each key sorts ascending by default, but may be reversed with the 'desc' modifier. Example: 'modifiedTime desc,name'.

pageSize.title: Page Size
pageSize.description: The maximum number of files to return per page.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page.

supportsAllDrives.title: Supports All Drives
supportsAllDrives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. If 'includeItemsFromAllDrives' is true, this must also be true.

includeItemsFromAllDrives.title: Include Items From All Drives
includeItemsFromAllDrives.description: Whether both My Drive and shared drive items should be included in results. If true, 'supportsAllDrives' should also be true.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string \"drive#fileList\".

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string \"drive#file\".

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the file's content in bytes. This is applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive and Google Docs files.

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is.

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user.

permissionId.title: Permission Id
permissionId.description: The user's permission ID.

owners.title: Owners
owners.description: The owners of the file. Currently, only certain legacy files may have more than one owner.

shared.title: Shared
shared.description: Whether the file has been shared.

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: The list of spaces which contain the file. The currently supported values are 'drive', 'appDataFolder' and 'photos'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

trashed.title: Trashed
trashed.description: Whether the file has been trashed, either explicitly or from a trashed parent folder.

version.title: Version
version.description: A monotonically increasing version number for the file. This reflects every change made to the file on the server, even those not visible to the user.

iconLink.title: Icon Link
iconLink.description: A static, unauthenticated link to the file's icon.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

ownedByMe.title: Owned By Me
ownedByMe.description: Whether the user owns the file.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are private to the requesting app.

viewedByMe.title: Viewed By Me
viewedByMe.description: Whether the file has been viewed by this user.

createdTime.title: Created Time
createdTime.description: The time at which the file was created.

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the file.

exportLinks.title: Export Links
exportLinks.description: Links for exporting Google Docs to specific formats.

md5Checksum.title: Md5 Checksum
md5Checksum.description: The MD5 checksum for the content of this file. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role granted by this permission.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the grantee.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user or group to which this permission refers.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user or group to which this permission refers.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The full list of permissions for the file.

resourceKey.title: Resource Key
resourceKey.description: A resource key for the file. If omitted, the requestor must have access to the file.

sharingUser.title: GoogleDriveUser

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is.

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user.

readOnly.description: Whether the content of this file is read-only.

viewersCanCopyContent.description: Whether users with viewer permissions can copy the content of this file.

incompleteSearch.title: Incomplete Search

successful.title: Successful
