human_description: This action allows you to search for files and folders in Google Drive. You can specify various query parameters to filter and sort the results, enabling you to quickly find the content you are looking for.

name: Find folder
description: Tool to find a folder in google drive by its name and optionally a parent folder. use when you need to locate a specific folder to perform further actions like creating files in it or listing its contents.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Set to true to search for folders that are starred, or false for those that are not.

name_exact.title: Name Exact
name_exact.description: The exact name of the folder to search for. This search is case-sensitive.

name_contains.title: Name Contains
name_contains.description: A substring to search for within folder names. This search is case-insensitive.

modified_after.title: Modified After
modified_after.description: Search for folders modified after a specific date and time. The timestamp must be in RFC 3339 format (e.g., '2023-01-15T10:00:00Z' or '2023-01-15T10:00:00.000Z').

name_not_contains.title: Name Not Contains
name_not_contains.description: A substring to exclude from folder names. Folders with names containing this substring will not be returned. This search is case-insensitive.

full_text_contains.title: Full Text Contains
full_text_contains.description: A string to search for within the full text content of files within folders (if applicable and supported by Drive for the folder type or its contents). This search is case-insensitive.

full_text_not_contains.title: Full Text Not Contains
full_text_not_contains.description: A string to exclude from the full text content of files within folders. This search is case-insensitive.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#fileList".

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#file".

shared.title: Shared
shared.description: Whether the file has been shared.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file.

trashed.title: Trashed
trashed.description: Whether the file has been trashed.

iconLink.title: Icon Link
iconLink.description: A static, unauthenticated link to the folder's icon.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the item. For a folder, this is 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'.

createdTime.title: Created Time
createdTime.description: The time at which the folder was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

teamDriveId.title: Team Drive Id
teamDriveId.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

webViewLink.title: Web View Link
webViewLink.description: A link for opening the folder in a browser.

capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.description: Capabilities the current user has on this folder.

modifiedTime.title: Modified Time
modifiedTime.description: The last time the folder was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time).

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: The page token for the next page of folders.

incompleteSearch.title: Incomplete Search
incompleteSearch.description: Whether the search process was incomplete.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action searches for folders in Google Drive based on specified name criteria. Users can filter folders by exact name, substrings, content, and modification dates. All search parameters must be provided as strings, not numbers.

name: Find free slots
description: Finds both free and busy time slots in google calendars for specified calendars within a defined time range (defaults to the current day utc if `time min`/`time max` are omitted). returns busy intervals enriched with event details and calculates free slots by finding gaps between busy periods; `time min` must precede `time max` if both are provided.

items.title: Items
items.description: List of calendar identifiers (primary ID 'primary', user/calendar email, or unique calendar ID) to query for free/busy information.

time_max.title: Time Max
time_max.description: End datetime for the query interval. Accepts ISO, comma-separated, or simple datetime formats.

time_min.title: Time Min
time_min.description: Start datetime for the query interval. Accepts ISO, comma-separated, or simple datetime formats.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London') for interpreting `time_min` and `time_max` if they lack timezone info, and for expanding recurring events.

group_expansion_max.title: Group Expansion Max
group_expansion_max.description: Maximum calendar identifiers to return for a single group; exceeding this causes an error. Max allowed: 100.

calendar_expansion_max.title: Calendar Expansion Max
calendar_expansion_max.description: Maximum calendars for which FreeBusy information is provided. Max allowed: 50.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Free/busy schedule for requested calendars, with busy intervals enriched with event details and calculated free slots based on gaps between busy periods.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves free and busy time slots for the specified calendars over a given time period. It analyzes the busy intervals from the calendars and provides calculated free slots based on the gaps in the busy periods.

name: Find worksheet by title
description: Finds a worksheet by its exact, case-sensitive title within a google spreadsheet; returns a boolean indicating if found and the complete metadata of the entire spreadsheet, regardless of whether the target worksheet is found.

title.title: Title
title.description: The exact, case-sensitive title of the worksheet (tab name) to find.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet. This ID is part of the spreadsheet's URL.

found.title: Found
found.description: True if a worksheet with the exact specified title was found within the spreadsheet; False otherwise.

sheet_data.title: Sheet Data
sheet_data.description: A dictionary containing the complete metadata of the Google Spreadsheet, including properties, named ranges, and details for all its worksheets (tabs). This data is provided regardless of whether the target worksheet is found.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Finds a worksheet by its title in a Google Spreadsheet. It checks if the exact title exists and returns the matched worksheet's metadata when found, or indicates not found with minimal response.

name: Format cell
description: Applies text and background cell formatting to a specified range in a google sheets worksheet.

input_parameters.title: FormatCellRequest

red.title: Red
red.description: Red component of the background color (0.0-1.0).

blue.title: Blue
blue.description: Blue component of the background color (0.0-1.0).

green.title: Green
green.description: Green component of the background color (0.0-1.0).

worksheet_id.title: Worksheet Id
worksheet_id.description: ID (sheetId) of the worksheet. Use `GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO` to find this ID.

end_row_index.title: End Row Index
end_row_index.description: 0-based index of the row *after* the last row in the range (exclusive); must be greater than `start_row_index`.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: Identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet.

start_row_index.title: Start Row Index
start_row_index.description: 0-based index of the first row in the range.

end_column_index.title: End Column Index
end_column_index.description: 0-based index of the column *after* the last column in the range (exclusive); must be greater than `start_column_index`.

start_column_index.title: Start Column Index
start_column_index.description: 0-based index of the first column in the range.

input_parameters.description: Parameters for applying formatting to a cell range in a Google Sheet.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: List of responses from the batch update; for cell formatting, typically one empty reply confirming the operation.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: Identifier of the spreadsheet where formatting was applied.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action formats a specific range of cells in a Google Sheet. It allows the user to apply various text and background styling options to enhance the appearance of the selected cells.

name: Forward email message
description: Forward an existing gmail message to specified recipients, preserving original body and attachments.

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

message_id.title: Message Id
message_id.description: ID of the message to forward, obtainable from actions like 'List Messages'.

additional_text.title: Additional Text
examples: Please see the forwarded message below.
additional_text.description: Optional additional text to include before the forwarded content.

recipient_email.title: Recipient Email
recipient_email.description: Email address to forward the message to.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Gmail API response with forwarded message details, including ID and threadId.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows users to forward an existing Gmail message to one or more specified recipients. It retains the original message body and attachments while allowing for optional additional text to be included in the forwarded message.

name: Query Free/Busy Information
description: Returns free/busy information for a set of calendars.

id.description: The identifier of a calendar or a group.

items.title: Items
items.description: List of calendars and/or groups to query.

timeMax.title: Time Max
timeMax.description: The end of the interval for the query formatted as per RFC3339.

timeMin.title: Time Min
timeMin.description: The start of the interval for the query formatted as per RFC3339.

timeZone.title: Time Zone
timeZone.description: Time zone used in the response. Optional. The default is UTC.

groupExpansionMax.title: Group Expansion Max
groupExpansionMax.description: Maximal number of calendar identifiers to be provided for a single group. Optional. An error is returned for a group with more members than this value. Maximum value is 100.

calendarExpansionMax.title: Calendar Expansion Max
calendarExpansionMax.description: Maximal number of calendars for which FreeBusy information is to be provided. Optional. Maximum value is 50.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource ("calendar#freeBusy").

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Domain, or broad category, of the error.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Optional error(s) (if computation for the group failed).

calendars.title: Calendars
calendars.description: List of calendars' identifiers within a group.

calendars.title: Calendars
calendars.description: List of free/busy information for calendars.

end.title: End
end.description: The (exclusive) end of the time period.

start.title: Start
start.description: The (inclusive) start of the time period.

busy.title: Busy
busy.description: List of time ranges during which this calendar should be regarded as busy.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Domain, or broad category, of the error.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Optional error(s) (if computation for the calendar failed).

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Generate File IDs
description: Generates a set of file ids which can be provided in create or copy requests. use when you need to pre-allocate ids for new files or copies.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of items for which the IDs can be used. For example, 'files' or 'shortcuts'.

count.title: Count
count.description: The number of IDs to return. Value must be between 1 and 1000, inclusive.

space.title: Space
space.description: The space in which the IDs can be used. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'.

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: The IDs generated by the server.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: "drive#idList".

space.title: Space
space.description: The space which is supported for these IDs.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
This action generates unique file IDs for use in create or copy requests. 
    It helps in pre-allocating IDs for new files or copies in the specified drive space.

name: Geocoding, Reverse Geocoding, and Place ID Lookup
description: Deprecated: tool to convert addresses into geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude) and vice versa (reverse geocoding), or get an address for a place id. use for finding coordinates for an address, finding an address for coordinates, or looking up an address by its place id.

key.title: Key
key.description: Your application's API key. This key identifies your application for purposes of quota management.

bounds.title: Bounds
bounds.description: The bounding box of the viewport within which to bias geocode results more prominently (e.g., '34.172684,-118.604794|34.236144,-118.500938'). This parameter will only influence, not fully restrict, results.

latlng.title: Latlng
latlng.description: The latitude and longitude coordinates specifying the location for which you want the closest, human-readable address (e.g., '40.714224,-73.961452'). Provide for reverse geocoding.

region.title: Region
region.description: The region code, specified as a ccTLD ('top-level domain') two-character value. This parameter will only influence, not fully restrict, results from the geocoder.

address.title: Address
address.description: The street address or plus code that you want to geocode. Provide for geocoding (address to coordinates).

language.title: Language
language.description: The language in which to return results. If language is not supplied, the geocoder attempts to use the preferred language as specified in the Accept-Language header, or the native language of the domain from which the request is sent.

place_id.title: Place Id
place_id.description: The place ID of the place for which you wish to obtain the human-readable address. Provide for place ID geocoding.

components.title: Components
components.description: A components filter with elements separated by a pipe (|). E.g., 'postal_code:94043|country:US'. Used for geocoding, can be restrictive or biasing.

result_type.title: Result Type
result_type.description: A filter of one or more address types, separated by a pipe (|) (e.g., 'street_address|locality'). Used for reverse geocoding and place ID geocoding.

location_type.title: Location Type
location_type.description: A filter of one or more location types, separated by a pipe (|) (e.g., 'ROOFTOP|RANGE_INTERPOLATED'). Used for reverse geocoding and place ID geocoding.

extra_computations.title: Extra Computations
extra_computations.description: Use this parameter to specify additional features in the response. Can select multiple values.

status.title: Status
status.description: Contains metadata on the request. OK indicates success.

types.title: Types
types.description: Array indicating the type of the returned result.

place_id.title: Place Id
place_id.description: A unique identifier for a place, which can be used with other Google APIs.

plus_code.title: PlusCodeModel
plus_code.description: An encoded location reference, derived from latitude and longitude coordinates.

partial_match.title: Partial Match
partial_match.description: Indicates that the geocoder did not return an exact match for the original request.

formatted_address.title: Formatted Address
formatted_address.description: String containing the human-readable address of this location.

address_components.title: Address Components
address_components.description: Array containing the separate components applicable to this address.

results.title: Results
results.description: An array of geocoded address information.

building_info.title: Building Info
building_info.description: Building and entrance information, if requested via extra_computations. Structure may vary.

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: More detailed information about the reasons behind a non-OK status code.

address_descriptors.title: Address Descriptors
address_descriptors.description: Address descriptors, if requested via extra_computations. Structure may vary.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

deprecated.displayName: Geocoding, Reverse Geocoding, and Place ID Lookup

name: Get directions
description: Fetches detailed directions between an origin and a destination, supporting intermediate waypoints and various travel modes.

input_parameters.title: GetDirectionRequest

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The mode of transportation for which to calculate directions. Supported values are 'driving' (default), 'walking', 'bicycling', and 'transit'.

avoid.title: Avoid
avoid.description: Specifies features to avoid in the generated route. Multiple values can be combined using a pipe delimiter (e.g., 'tolls|highways'). Valid options include 'tolls', 'highways', and 'ferries'.

units.title: Units
units.description: The unit system for displaying distances. Supported values are 'metric' (kilometers and meters) and 'imperial' (miles and feet). Defaults to 'imperial'.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The starting point for the directions. This can be a textual address (e.g., '123 Main St, Los Angeles, CA'), a place name (e.g., 'Disneyland'), or latitude/longitude coordinates (e.g., '34.0522,-118.2437').

language.title: Language
language.description: The language code for returning results, e.g., 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish. Defaults to 'en'.

waypoints.title: Waypoints
waypoints.description: A comma-separated string of intermediate locations (addresses, place names, or coordinates) to visit between the origin and destination.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: The ending point for the directions. This can be a textual address (e.g., '456 Park Ave, New York, NY'), a place name (e.g., 'Universal Studios Hollywood'), or latitude/longitude coordinates (e.g., '40.7128,-74.0060').

input_parameters.description: Parameters for requesting directions between specified locations.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: The raw JSON response data from the Google Maps Directions API, containing detailed route information including legs, steps, duration, distance, and geometry.

description: Retrieves either a specific person's details (using `resource name`) or lists 'other contacts' (if `other contacts` is true), with `person fields` specifying the data to return.

sync_token.description: A token from a previous 'Other Contacts' list call to retrieve only changes since the last sync; leave empty for an initial full sync. Applicable only when `other_contacts` is true.

person_fields.title: Person Fields
person_fields.description: A comma-separated field mask to restrict which fields on the person (or persons) are returned. Consult the Google People API documentation for a comprehensive list of valid fields.

people_data.title: People Data
people_data.description: Contains the person's data as requested, populated when `other_contacts` is false and a single person is fetched.

other_contacts.title: Other Contacts

supports_all_drives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

use_domain_admin_access.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

id.title: Id
id.description: The ID of the permission. This is a unique identifier for the grantee, and is published in User resources as permissionId.

kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#permission".

role.title: Role
role.description: The role granted by this permission. While new values may be supported in the future, the following are currently possible: "owner", "organizer", "fileOrganizer", "writer", "commenter", "reader"

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the grantee. Valid values are: "user", "group", "domain", "anyone"

view.title: View
view.description: Indicates the view for this permission. Only populated for permissions that belong to a view. published is the only supported value.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain to which this permission refers.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Whether the account associated with this permission has been deleted. This field only pertains to user and group permissions.

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: A displayable name for users, groups or domains.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user or group to which this permission refers.

expirationTime.title: Expiration Time
expirationTime.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time). Expiration times have the following restrictions: - They can only be set on user and group permissions - The time must be in the future - The time cannot be more than a year in the future

permissionDetails.title: Permission Details
permissionDetails.description: Details of whether the permissions on this Team Drive item are inherited or directly on this item. This is an output-only field which is present only for Team Drive items.

allowFileDiscovery.title: Allow File Discovery
allowFileDiscovery.description: Whether the permission allows the file to be discovered through search. This is only applicable for permissions of type domain or anyone.

teamDrivePermissionDetails.title: Team Drive Permission Details
teamDrivePermissionDetails.description: Details of whether the permissions on this shared drive item are inherited or directly on this item. This is an output-only field which is present only for shared drive items.

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful

name: Get Revision
description: Tool to get a specific revision's metadata by revision id. use when you need to retrieve information about a particular version of a file.

acknowledge_abuse.title: Acknowledge Abuse
acknowledge_abuse.description: Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. This is only applicable when the alt parameter is set to media and the user is the owner of the file or an organizer of the shared drive in which the file resides.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#revision".

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the revision content in bytes. This is only populated for revisions of files with binary content in Drive.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: The MIME type of the revision.

published.title: Published
published.description: Whether this revision is published. This is only populated for files with Google Docs Editors content types.

export_links.title: Export Links
export_links.description: Links for exporting Docs Editors files to specific formats.

keep_forever.title: Keep Forever
keep_forever.description: Whether to keep this revision forever, even if it is no longer the head revision. If not set or false, the revision will be automatically purged 30 days after newer revisions are created.

md5_checksum.title: Md5 Checksum
md5_checksum.description: The MD5 checksum of the revision. This is only populated for revisions of files with binary content in Drive.

publish_link.title: Publish Link
publish_link.description: A link to the published revision.

modified_time.title: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The last time the revision was modified (RFC 3339 date-time).

original_filename.title: Original Filename
original_filename.description: The original filename of the revision. This is only populated for revisions of files with binary content in Drive.

last_modifying_user.title: Last Modifying User
last_modifying_user.description: The user who last modified the revision.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves metadata for a specific revision of a file by its revision ID. It's useful for obtaining details such as the modification time, size, and state of the revision.

name: Get Route
description: Calculates one or more routes between two specified locations using various travel modes and preferences; addresses must be resolvable by google maps.

input_parameters.title: GetRouteRequest

units.title: Units
units.description: Unit system (e.g., 'METRIC' for kilometers, 'IMPERIAL' for miles) for displaying distances.

fieldMask.title: Field Mask
fieldMask.description: Comma-separated list of `Route` object fields to include in the response (e.g., 'routes.distanceMeters,routes.duration') for efficiency.

travelMode.title: Travel Mode
travelMode.description: Mode of transportation for the route.

languageCode.title: Language Code
languageCode.description: BCP-47 language code (e.g., 'en-US', 'es') for textual information like navigation instructions.

origin_address.title: Origin Address
origin_address.description: Starting point address or place name for the route calculation.

routingPreference.title: Routing Preference
routingPreference.description: Specifies routing preference: `TRAFFIC_UNAWARE` (fastest, ignores traffic), `TRAFFIC_AWARE` (considers traffic, optimized), `TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL` (most accurate traffic-based routing), or `ROUTING_PREFERENCE_UNSPECIFIED` (behavior similar to `TRAFFIC_UNAWARE`).

destination_address.title: Destination Address
destination_address.description: Destination point address or place name for the route calculation.

computeAlternativeRoutes.title: Compute Alternative Routes
computeAlternativeRoutes.description: Computes and returns alternative routes if true.

routeModifiers_avoidTolls.title: Route Modifiers Avoid Tolls
routeModifiers_avoidTolls.description: Attempts to avoid toll roads if true.

routeModifiers_avoidFerries.title: Route Modifiers Avoid Ferries
routeModifiers_avoidFerries.description: Attempts to avoid ferries if true.

routeModifiers_avoidHighways.title: Route Modifiers Avoid Highways
routeModifiers_avoidHighways.description: Attempts to avoid highways if true.

input_parameters.description: Parameters for requesting a route, including origin, destination, travel mode, and preferences.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Raw JSON response from the Google Maps Routes API, structured based on `fieldMask`, typically including route legs, distance, duration, and polylines.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Get Table Schema
description: This action is used to get the schema of a table in a google spreadsheet, call this action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet before you query the table. analyze table structure and infer column names, types, and constraints. uses statistical analysis of sample data to determine the most likely data type for each column. call this action after calling the list tables action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet.

has_header.title: Has Header

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Insert Dimension in Google Sheet
description: Tool to insert new rows or columns into a sheet at a specified location. use when you need to add empty rows or columns within an existing google sheet.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

response_ranges.description: Limits the ranges of the spreadsheet to include in the response.

sheet_id.title: Sheet Id
sheet_id.description: The ID of the sheet where the dimensions will be inserted.

dimension.title: Dimension
dimension.description: The dimension to insert. Valid values are "ROWS" or "COLUMNS".

end_index.title: End Index
end_index.description: The end index (0-based, exclusive) of the dimension range to insert. The number of rows/columns to insert is `endIndex - startIndex`.

start_index.title: Start Index
start_index.description: The start index (0-based) of the dimension range to insert. The inserted dimensions will be placed before this index.

inherit_from_before.title: Inherit From Before
inherit_from_before.description: If true, the new dimensions will inherit properties from the dimension before the startIndex. If false (default), they will inherit from the dimension at the startIndex. startIndex must be greater than 0 if inheritFromBefore is true.

insert_dimension.description: The details for the insert dimension request.

response_include_grid_data.title: Response Include Grid Data
response_include_grid_data.description: True if grid data should be included in the response (if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true).

include_spreadsheet_in_response.title: Include Spreadsheet In Response
include_spreadsheet_in_response.description: True if the updated spreadsheet should be included in the response.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies to the batch update requests. For InsertDimension, the reply is usually empty on success.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet that was updated.

updatedSpreadsheet.title: Spreadsheet
updatedSpreadsheet.description: The updated spreadsheet resource, if requested via includeSpreadsheetInResponse.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

location.title: Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index within the segment where the image will be inserted. If not provided, the image is inserted at the end of the segment.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the segment where the image will be inserted. If not provided, the image is inserted in the document body.

location.description: The location in the document to insert the image.

objectSize.title: ObjectSize

unit.title: UnitEnum
unit.description: The units for magnitude. Supported units: EMU, PT, PX.

objectSize.description: The size of the image. If not specified, the image is rendered at its intrinsic size.

status.title: Status
status.description: Indicates the status of the operation.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document that was updated.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document to update.

insertPageBreak.title: Insert Page Break

location.title: Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units. The page break is inserted before this index.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the segment where the page break is to be inserted. For page breaks, this should typically be null or empty, indicating the main document body, as page breaks are not allowed in headers, footers, or footnotes.

location.description: Specifies the location to insert the page break using a zero-based index. Use this or end_of_segment_location.

endOfSegmentLocation.title: EndOfSegmentLocation

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the segment where the page break is to be inserted. For page breaks, this must be null or an empty string, indicating the end of the main document body.

endOfSegmentLocation.description: Specifies that the page break should be inserted at the end of a segment (document body). Use this or location.

insertPageBreak.description: The details for the insertPageBreak request.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies, one for each request made. For a single insert page break, this will contain one reply.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document that was updated.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

rows.title: Rows

index.description: The zero-based index where the table will be inserted. If provided, 'location' will be used. If omitted and 'insertAtEndOfSegment' is false or omitted, 'endOfSegmentLocation' will be used for the document body.

insertAtEndOfSegment.description: If true, inserts the table at the end of the segment (document body, header, or footer specified by segment_id). If false or omitted, and 'index' is not provided, it defaults to inserting at the end of the document body. If 'index' is provided, this field is ignored.

replies.title: Replies

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document that the table was inserted into.

successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

columnIndex.title: Column Index

name: Insert Task
description: Creates a new task in a given `tasklist id`, optionally as a subtask of an existing `task parent` or positioned after an existing `task previous` sibling, where both `task parent` and `task previous` must belong to the same `tasklist id` if specified.

input_parameters.title: InsertTaskRequest

due.title: Due
due.description:
Due date of the task. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Notes describing the task. Optional. Maximum length allowed: 8192 characters.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

hidden.title: Hidden
hidden.description: Flag indicating whether the task is hidden.

status.title: Status
status.description: Status of the task. This is either 'needsAction' or 'completed'.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Flag indicating whether the task has been deleted.

completed.title: Completed
completed.description:
Completion date of the task. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

task_parent.title: Task Parent
task_parent.description: Identifier of an existing task to serve as parent; if provided, creates a subtask, otherwise a top-level task in the specified list.

tasklist_id.title: Tasklist Id
tasklist_id.description: Identifier for the Google Task list where the new task will be created.

task_previous.title: Task Previous
task_previous.description: Identifier of an existing task after which the new task will be placed, at the same hierarchical level (as a sibling).

input_parameters.description:
Defines the request for creating a new task. Inherits common task properties
(e.g., title, notes, due date) from `TaskInput` and adds specific parameters
for task placement within a Google Tasks list.

task.title: Task
task.description: A dictionary representing the complete Google Task resource that was newly created, including all its properties as returned by the API, if the operation was successful.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

The index is zero-based and refers to the UTF-16 code unit. For example, to insert text at the beginning of the document, use an index of 1 (to account for the first paragraph).

name: List ACL Rules
description: Retrieves the list of access control rules (acls) for a specified calendar, providing the necessary 'rule id' values required for updating specific acl rules.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

sync_token.title: Sync Token
sync_token.description: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of a previous list operation. It makes the result of this list operation contain only entries that have changed since then. Optional. The default is to retrieve all entries.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. Optional. The default is 100.

show_deleted.title: Show Deleted
show_deleted.description: Whether to include deleted ACLs in the result. Optional. The default is False.

kind.title: Kind

role.title: Role
role.description: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: "none", "freeBusyReader", "reader", "writer", "owner".

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the scope. Possible values are: "default", "user", "group", "domain".

value.title: Value
value.description: The email address or domain name in the scope. Optional.

items.title: Items
items.description: List of rules on the access control list.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result. Omitted if the backend doesn't support query token.

nextSyncToken.title: Next Sync Token
nextSyncToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result. Omitted if the backend doesn't support query token.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Retrieves access control rules for a specified calendar, allowing users to see the permissions associated with different users and groups.
    This action helps in managing who can view or edit calendar events effectively.

name: List Changes
description: Tool to list the changes for a user or shared drive. use when you need to track modifications to files and folders, such as creations, deletions, or permission changes. this action requires a `pagetoken` which can be initially obtained using the `get changes start page token` action or from a previous `list changes` response.

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpora. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The shared drive from which changes will be returned. If specified the change IDs will be reflective of the shared drive; use the combined drive ID and change ID as an identifier.

pageSize.title: Page Size
pageSize.description: The maximum number of changes to return per page.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of 'nextPageToken' from the previous response or to the response from the getStartPageToken method.

includeLabels.title: Include Labels
includeLabels.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response.

includeRemoved.title: Include Removed
includeRemoved.description: Whether to include changes indicating that items have been removed from the list of changes, for example by deletion or loss of access.

restrictToMyDrive.title: Restrict To My Drive
restrictToMyDrive.description: Whether to restrict the results to changes inside the My Drive hierarchy. This omits changes to files such as those in the Application Data folder or shared files which have not been added to My Drive.

supportsAllDrives.title: Supports All Drives
supportsAllDrives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

includeCorpusRemovals.title: Include Corpus Removals
includeCorpusRemovals.description: Whether changes should include the file resource if the file is still accessible by the user at the time of the request, even when a file was removed from the list of changes and there will be no further change entries for this file.

includeItemsFromAllDrives.title: Include Items From All Drives
includeItemsFromAllDrives.description: Whether both My Drive and shared drive items should be included in results.

includePermissionsForView.title: Include Permissions For View
includePermissionsForView.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#changeList".

file.title: File
file.description: The updated state of the file. Present if the type is file and the file has not been removed from this list of changes.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#change".

time.title: Time
time.description: The time of this change (RFC 3339 date-time).

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the change. Possible values are "file" and "drive".

drive.title: Drive
drive.description: The updated state of the shared drive. Present if the type is drive and the shared drive has not been removed from this list of changes.

fileId.title: File Id
fileId.description: The ID of the file which has changed.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive associated with this change.

removed.title: Removed
removed.description: Whether the file or shared drive has been removed from this list of changes, for example by deletion or loss of access.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: The list of changes. If nextPageToken is populated, then this list may be incomplete and an additional page of results should be fetched.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: The page token for the next page of changes. This will be absent if the end of the changes list has been reached. The page token doesn't expire.

newStartPageToken.title: New Start Page Token
newStartPageToken.description: The starting page token for future changes. This will be present only if the end of the current changes list has been reached. The page token doesn't expire.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action retrieves a list of changes for a user or shared drive. It allows tracking modifications to files and folders, such as creations, deletions, or permission changes.

includeDeleted.title: Include Deleted

startModifiedTime.title: Start Modified Time

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

permissionId.title: Permission Id

author.description: The author of the comment. The author's email address and permission ID will not be populated.

content.title: Content
content.description: The plain text content of the comment.

author.title: CommentAuthor

me.title: Me

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

permissionId.title: Permission Id

author.description: The author of the reply. The author's email address and permission ID will not be populated.

replies.title: Replies

Examples: `space.name = "spaces/NAME"`, `space.meeting_code = "abc-mnop-xyz"`, `start_time>="2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z" AND start_time<="2024-01-02T00:00:00.000Z"`, `end_time IS NULL`

page_size.title: Page Size

expire_time.title: Expire Time

conference_records.title: Conference Records

name: List Files and Folders
description: Tool to list a user's files and folders in google drive. use this to search or browse for files and folders based on various criteria.

q.title: Q
q.description: A query string for filtering the file results. Supports operators like 'and', 'or', 'not', and can filter on fields like 'name', 'mimeType', 'modifiedTime', 'starred', 'trashed'. Example: "name contains 'important' and mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'".

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. This can be used to retrieve only specific file metadata, improving performance. Example: 'nextPageToken, files(id, name, mimeType, modifiedTime)'

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpora. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'. 'drive' represents files in My Drive and shared drives, while 'appDataFolder' represents the application's private data folder.

corpora.title: Corpora
corpora.description: Specifies the bodies of items (files/documents) to which the query applies. Supported values are 'user', 'domain', 'drive', and 'allDrives'. It's generally more efficient to use 'user' or 'drive' instead of 'allDrives'. Defaults to 'user'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive to search. This is used when `corpora` is set to 'drive'.

orderBy.title: Order By
orderBy.description: A comma-separated list of sort keys. Valid keys include 'createdTime', 'folder', 'modifiedByMeTime', 'modifiedTime', 'name', 'name_natural', 'quotaBytesUsed', 'recency', 'sharedWithMeTime', 'starred', 'viewedByMeTime'. Each key sorts in ascending order by default, but can be reversed with the 'desc' modifier (e.g., 'modifiedTime desc').

folderId.title: Folder Id
folderId.description: ID of a specific folder to list files from. This is a convenience parameter that automatically adds "'folder_id' in parents" to the query. Cannot be used together with a custom 'q' parameter.

pageSize.title: Page Size
pageSize.description: The maximum number of files to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 1000, inclusive. Defaults to 100.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of 'nextPageToken' from the previous response.

includeLabels.title: Include Labels
includeLabels.description: A comma-separated list of label IDs to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response for each file.

supportsAllDrives.title: Supports All Drives
supportsAllDrives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to false. If true, then `includeItemsFromAllDrives` can be used to extend the search to all drives.

includeItemsFromAllDrives.title: Include Items From All Drives
includeItemsFromAllDrives.description: Whether to include items from both My Drive and shared drives. This is relevant when `corpora` is 'user' or 'domain'. Defaults to false.

includePermissionsForView.title: Include Permissions For View
includePermissionsForView.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Currently, only 'published' is supported, which includes permissions for files with published content.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource as a file list, typically 'drive#fileList'.

files.title: Files
files.description: A list of file resources. Each file resource contains details like 'id', 'name', 'mimeType', 'createdTime', 'modifiedTime', etc.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: The page token for the next page of results. This will be absent if the end of the results is reached.

incompleteSearch.title: Incomplete Search
incompleteSearch.description: Whether the search process was incomplete. True if the entire result set could not be retrieved before the timeout.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: List Participant Sessions
description: Tool to list all participant sessions for a specific conference record in google meet. use this when you need to retrieve a list of participants who joined a particular meeting.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: Optional. User-specified filtering condition in EBNF format. Filterable fields include `earliest_start_time` and `latest_end_time`.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Required. The identifier of the conference record, in the format `conferenceRecords/{conferenceRecord}`.

page_size.title: Page Size
page_size.description: Optional. Maximum number of participants to return. The service might return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 100 participants are returned. The maximum value is 250; values above 250 are coerced to 250.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Optional. Page token returned from a previous list call, used to retrieve the next page of results.

total_size.title: Total Size
total_size.description: Total, exact number of `participants`. By default, this field isn't included in the response. Set the field mask in [SystemParameterContext](https://cloud.google.com/apis/docs/system-parameters) to receive this field in the response.

phone_user.title: Phone User
phone_user.description: Output only. The user that is dialing in by phone at the beginning of the session. This is only populated if the user is dialing in by phone, and if the user grants the oAuth scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.profile.readonly`.

participant.title: Participant
participant.description: Output only. The unique participant ID.

anonymous_user.title: Anonymous User
anonymous_user.description: Output only. The anonymous user that is at the beginning of the session. This is only populated if the user is not signed-in.

signed_in_user.title: Signed In User
signed_in_user.description: Output only. The user that is signed-in at the beginning of the session. This is only populated if the user is signed-in, and if the user grants the oAuth scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/meetings.space.profile.readonly`.

latest_end_time.title: Latest End Time
latest_end_time.description: Output only. Time when the participant left the meeting for the last time. This can be null if the participant is still in the meeting.

earliest_start_time.title: Earliest Start Time
earliest_start_time.description: Output only. Time when the participant first joined the meeting.

participants.title: Participants
participants.description: List of participants in one page.

next_page_token.title: Next Page Token
next_page_token.description: Token to be circulated back for further List call if current List doesn't include all the participants. Unset if all participants are returned.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

includePermissionsForView.title: Include Permissions For View
includePermissionsForView.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

name: List File Revisions

pageSize.title: Page Size

kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#revision".

mimeType.title: Mime Type

exportLinks.title: Export Links

keepForever.title: Keep Forever

publishAuto.title: Publish Auto

modifiedTime.title: Modified Time

publishedLink.title: Published Link

emailAddress.title: Email Address

permissionId.title: Permission Id
permissionId.description: The ETag of the permission.

lastModifyingUser.description: The last user to modify this revision.

revisions.title: Revisions

successful.title: Successful

name: List Tasks
description: Retrieves tasks from a google tasks list; all date/time strings must be rfc3339 utc, and `showcompleted` must be true if `completedmin` or `completedmax` are specified.

dueMax.title: Due Max
dueMax.description:
Exclude tasks due after this date. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

dueMin.title: Due Min
dueMin.description:
Exclude tasks due before this date. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: Token from a previous list operation for fetching a specific page; if omitted, retrieves the first page.

maxResults.title: Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of tasks to return. API default: 20, maximum: 100.

showHidden.title: Show Hidden
showHidden.description: Include hidden tasks. Defaults to false.

updatedMin.title: Updated Min
updatedMin.description:
Lower bound for task's last modification time (for syncing). 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

showDeleted.title: Show Deleted
showDeleted.description: Include deleted tasks. Defaults to false.

tasklist_id.title: Tasklist Id
tasklist_id.description: Identifier of the task list. Use '@default' for the user's primary task list.

completedMax.title: Completed Max
completedMax.description:
Exclude tasks completed after this date. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

completedMin.title: Completed Min
completedMin.description:
Exclude tasks completed before this date. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

showCompleted.title: Show Completed
showCompleted.description: Include completed tasks. Defaults to true. (See action docstring for interaction with `completedMin`/`Max`).

due.title: Due
due.description:
Due date of the task. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource. This is always 'tasks#task'.

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of the link, e.g. 'email'.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the link.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Notes describing the task. Optional. Maximum length allowed: 8192 characters.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

hidden.title: Hidden
hidden.description: Flag indicating whether the task is hidden.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Parent task identifier. This field is omitted if it is a top-level task.

status.title: Status
status.description: Status of the task. This is either 'needsAction' or 'completed'.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Flag indicating whether the task has been deleted.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Last modification time of the task (as a RFC 3339 timestamp).

position.title: Position
position.description: String indicating the position of the task among its sibling tasks.

selfLink.title: Self Link
selfLink.description: URL pointing to this task. Used to retrieve, update, or delete this task.

completed.title: Completed
completed.description:
Completion date of the task. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

webViewLink.title: Web View Link
webViewLink.description: An absolute link to the task in the Google Tasks Web UI.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.description: List of tasks retrieved matching the specified criteria.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: Pagination token to retrieve the next page of results; omitted if no more tasks.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: List threads

user_id.title: User Id
user_id.description: The user's email address or 'me' to specify the authenticated Gmail account.

verbose.title: Verbose
verbose.description: If false, returns threads with basic fields (id, snippet, historyId). If true, returns threads with complete message details including headers, body, attachments, and metadata for each message in the thread.

page_token.title: Page Token
page_token.description: Token from a previous response to retrieve a specific page of results; omit for the first page.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of threads to return.

threads.title: Threads
threads.description: List of email thread resources, each usually containing 'id', 'snippet', and 'historyId'.

next_page_token.title: Next Page Token
next_page_token.description: Token for the next page of results; if present, use as `page_token` in a subsequent request to fetch more threads.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action fetches a list of email threads associated with a specific user. It provides options to filter results and paginate through them based on the specified parameters.

name: Look up spreadsheet row
description: Finds the first row in a google spreadsheet where a cell's entire content exactly matches the query string, searching within a specified a1 notation range or the first sheet by default.

query.title: Query
query.description: Exact text value to find; matches the entire content of a cell in a row.

range.title: Range
range.description: A1 notation of the range to search (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D5', 'MySheet!A:Z', or 'Sheet1'). Defaults to the non-empty part of the first sheet. For multiple sheets, include sheet name (e.g., 'SheetName!A1:Z100').

case_sensitive.title: Case Sensitive
case_sensitive.description: If `True`, the query string search is case-sensitive.

spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheet_id.description: Identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to search.

row_data.title: Row Data
row_data.description: Lookup outcome: a dictionary with 'found' (boolean for match identification) and 'row_data' (list of cell values from the first matched row, or empty if no match).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action searches for a specific text within a designated range of a Google Spreadsheet. It returns the first matching row where the entire content of a cell matches the provided query string.

name: Embed Google Map
description: Tool to generate an embeddable google map url and html iframe code. use when you need to display a map (place, view, directions, street view, search) on a webpage without javascript.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The mode of the embedded map.

zoom.title: Zoom
zoom.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

center.title: Center
center.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840'). This is REQUIRED for view mode.

region.title: Region
region.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

maptype.title: Maptype
maptype.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

language.title: Language
language.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

q.title: Q
q.description: Defines the map marker location. Accepts a URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

zoom.title: Zoom
zoom.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

center.title: Center
center.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

region.title: Region
region.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

maptype.title: Maptype
maptype.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

language.title: Language
language.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

q.title: Q
q.description: Defines the search term. Can include a geographic restriction (e.g., 'record+stores+in+Seattle').

zoom.title: Zoom
zoom.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

center.title: Center
center.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

region.title: Region
region.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

maptype.title: Maptype
maptype.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

language.title: Language
language.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

zoom.title: Zoom
zoom.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

avoid.title: Avoid
avoid.description: Specifies features to avoid in directions (e.g., 'tolls', 'highways', 'ferries'). Separate multiple values with the pipe character (|). E.g. 'tolls|highways'.

center.title: Center
center.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: Defines the starting point for directions. Accepts URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, lat/lng coordinates, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

region.title: Region
region.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

maptype.title: Maptype
maptype.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

language.title: Language
language.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

waypoints.title: Waypoints
waypoints.description: Specifies one or more intermediary places to route directions. Separate multiple waypoints with the pipe character (|). E.g., 'Berlin,Germany|Paris,France'. Up to 20 waypoints.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: Defines the endpoint of the directions. Accepts URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, lat/lng coordinates, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

fov.title: Fov
fov.description: Horizontal field of view in degrees (10-100). Smaller numbers mean higher zoom.

pano.title: Pano
pano.description: A specific panorama ID. Required if 'location' is not provided. 'location' can be a fallback.

zoom.title: Zoom
zoom.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

pitch.title: Pitch
pitch.description: Angle, up or down, of the camera in degrees (-90 to 90). Positive is up.

center.title: Center
center.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

radius.title: Radius
radius.description: Radius in meters to search for a panorama, centered on lat/lng. Default 50.

region.title: Region
region.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

source.title: Source
source.description: Limits Street View searches to selected sources. 'default' or 'outdoor'.

heading.title: Heading
heading.description: Compass heading of the camera in degrees clockwise from North (-180 to 360).

maptype.title: Maptype
maptype.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

language.title: Language
language.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

location.title: Location

name: Move File
description: Tool to move a file from one folder to another in google drive. use when you need to reorganize files by changing their parent folder(s).

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: The ID of the file to move.

add_parents.title: Add Parents
add_parents.description: A comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to add the file to. Use this to specify the destination folder.

ocr_language.title: Ocr Language
ocr_language.description: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

include_labels.title: Include Labels
include_labels.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response.

remove_parents.title: Remove Parents
remove_parents.description: A comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to remove the file from. Use this to specify the source folder.

supports_all_drives.title: Supports All Drives
supports_all_drives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Set to true if moving files to or from a shared drive.

keep_revision_forever.title: Keep Revision Forever
keep_revision_forever.description: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

include_permissions_for_view.title: Include Permissions For View
include_permissions_for_view.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

use_content_as_indexable_text.title: Use Content As Indexable Text
use_content_as_indexable_text.description: Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#file'.

parents.title: Parents

name: Move Task
description: Moves the specified task to another position in the destination task list.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: New parent task identifier. If not provided, the task is moved to the top level.

previous.title: Previous
previous.description: New previous sibling task identifier. If not provided, the task is moved to the first position among its siblings.

destinationTasklist.title: Destination Tasklist
destinationTasklist.description: Destination task list identifier. If set, the task is moved to this list; otherwise, it remains in its current list.

due.title: Due
due.description:
Due date of the task. 
Some examples of expected datetime format:
	UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM	->If date is not mentioned, and seeming that the start date is in next 24 hours.
	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

kind.title: Kind

notes.title: Notes

title.title: Title

hidden.title: Hidden

parent.title: Parent

status.title: Status

updated.title: Updated

completed.title: Completed

	UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec	->If the meeting start date is in next 1 year (this is most possible case)
	UTC-3:45, 7:15 AM, 22 Aug 2020	->If the meeting is to be set after at a spefic date, mentioned with year.
	11:15 PM, 12 May 2019	->In case no UTC offset to be mentioned, skip the UTC part altogether.
	2023-03-01T00:00:00Z	->Standard ISO-8601 format

message_id.title: Message Id

This action allows the user to move a specified email message to the trash.

name: Nearby search
description: Searches for places (e.g., restaurants, parks) within a specified circular area, with options to filter by place types and customize the returned fields and number of results.

radius.title: Radius
radius.description: Radius of the circular search area in meters.

latitude.title: Latitude
latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

fieldMask.title: Field Mask
fieldMask.description: Comma-separated list of place fields for the response (e.g., 'places.displayName,places.formattedAddress'); use '*' for all fields.

longitude.title: Longitude
longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

excludedTypes.title: Excluded Types
excludedTypes.description: Place types to exclude (e.g., 'cafe', 'store'); results matching any of these types are omitted. For supported types, see Google Maps Places API documentation.

includedTypes.title: Included Types
includedTypes.description: Place types to include (e.g., 'restaurant', 'park'); results will match at least one of these types. For supported types, see Google Maps Places API documentation.

maxResultCount.title: Max Result Count
maxResultCount.description: Maximum number of search results to return (up to 20); the actual count may be lower.

places.title: Places
places.description: List of found places matching search criteria; each place is a dictionary of requested fields.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

mime_type.title: MimeType

file.title: File

warning.title: Warning

successful.title: Successful
