name: Patch Calendar
description: Partially updates (patches) an existing google calendar, modifying only the fields provided; `summary` is mandatory and cannot be an empty string, and an empty string for `description` or `location` clears them.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: New title for the calendar; cannot be an empty string.

location.title: Location
location.description: New geographic location of the calendar (e.g., 'Paris, France').

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: New IANA Time Zone Database name for the calendar (e.g., 'Europe/Zurich', 'America/New_York').

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the Google Calendar to update; use 'primary' for the main calendar or a specific ID.

description.title: Description
description.description: New description for the calendar.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: A dictionary representing the full calendar resource that was updated, conforming to the Google Calendar API's calendar resource structure.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates specific details of a Google Calendar based on the provided inputs. Users can modify fields such as the title, description, location, and timezone of the calendar.

name: Patch Event
description: Updates specified fields of an existing event in a google calendar using patch semantics (array fields like `attendees` are fully replaced if provided); ensure the `calendar id` and `event id` are valid and the user has write access to the calendar.

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: New end time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T11:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format (exclusive end date).

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Identifier of the event to update.

location.title: Location
location.description: New geographic location (physical address or virtual meeting link).

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA Time Zone Database name for start/end times (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used if `start_time` and `end_time` are provided and not all-day dates; defaults to UTC if unset.

attendees.title: Attendees
attendees.description: List of email addresses for attendees. Replaces existing attendees. Provide an empty list to remove all.

start_time.title: Start Time
start_time.description: New start time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T10:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the calendar. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the logged-in user. To find other calendar IDs, use the `calendarList.list` method.

description.title: Description
examples: Weekly team sync meeting to discuss project updates.
description.description: New description for the event; can include HTML.

send_updates.title: Send Updates
send_updates.description: Whether to send update notifications to attendees: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. Uses default user behavior if unspecified.

max_attendees.title: Max Attendees
max_attendees.description: Maximum attendees in response; does not affect invited count. If more, response includes organizer only. Must be positive.

rsvp_response.title: Rsvp Response
rsvp_response.description: RSVP response status for the authenticated user. Updates only the current user's response status without affecting other attendees. Possible values: 'needsAction', 'declined', 'tentative', 'accepted'.

supports_attachments.title: Supports Attachments
supports_attachments.description: Client application supports event attachments. Set to `True` if so.

conference_data_version.title: Conference Data Version
conference_data_version.description: API client's conference data support version. Set to 1 to manage conference details (e.g., Google Meet links); 0 (default) ignores conference data.

end.title: End
end.description: Event end time: 'dateTime' (RFC3339 timestamp) or 'date' (YYYY-MM-DD for all-day, exclusive), and 'timeZone'.

start.title: Start
start.description: Event start time: 'dateTime' (RFC3339 timestamp) or 'date' (YYYY-MM-DD for all-day), and 'timeZone'.

creator.title: Creator
creator.description: User who created the event, including 'email' and 'displayName'.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Event last modification time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T09:30:00.000Z').

htmlLink.title: Html Link
htmlLink.description: URL to access the event in Google Calendar web interface.

attendees.title: Attendees
attendees.description: List of attendees, with details like 'email', 'displayName', 'responseStatus'.

organizer.title: Organizer
organizer.description: Event organizer, including 'email', 'displayName', and 'self' (boolean: is authenticated user the organizer).

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the event; may contain HTML.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates existing events in Google Calendar by modifying specified fields. It can change details like event time, title, and attendees as needed.

name: Query Spreadsheet Table
description: This action is used to query a table in a google spreadsheet, call this action to query a table in a spreadsheet. execute sql-like select queries against spreadsheet tables. supports where conditions, order by, limit clauses. call this action after calling the get table schema action to query a table in a spreadsheet.

examples: SELECT project, totals FROM "Sales_Data" WHERE totals > 10.0 ORDER BY totals DESC
SELECT name, email FROM "Customers" WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
sql.description: SQL SELECT query. Supported: SELECT cols FROM table WHERE conditions ORDER BY col LIMIT n. Table names must be quoted if they contain spaces.

spreadsheet_id.description: Google Sheets ID

include_formulas.title: Include Formulas
include_formulas.description: Whether to return formula text instead of calculated values for formula columns

name: Quick Add Event
description: Parses natural language text to quickly create a basic google calendar event with its title, date, and time, suitable for simple scheduling; does not support direct attendee addition or recurring events, and `calendar id` must be valid if not 'primary'.

examples: Dentist appointment tomorrow at 2pm for 45 minutes
text.description: Natural language input describing the event; Google Calendar parses this for event details like title, date, and time.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the calendar for the event. Use 'primary' for the main calendar, or provide a specific calendar ID (e.g., email address).

send_updates.title: Send Updates
send_updates.description: Controls whether email notifications about the event creation are sent to attendees.

event.title: Event
event.description: The Google Calendar event resource created by the API, including details such as 'id', 'summary' (title), 'start' and 'end' times/dates.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action takes a natural language description of an event and creates a corresponding event in Google Calendar. It allows for quick scheduling by parsing details such as the event title, date, and time.

This action allows you to remove a specific attendee from an event in your Google Calendar.

match_case.title: Match Case

This action replaces all instances of a given text in the specified document with a different text.

name: Replace Image in Document
description: Tool to replace a specific image in a document with a new image from a uri. use when you need to update an existing image within a google doc.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document containing the image to replace.

replace_image.title: Replace Image

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: The URI of the new image. The image is fetched once at insertion time and a copy is stored for display inside the document. Images must be less than 50MB in size, cannot exceed 25 megapixels, and must be in one of PNG, JPEG, or GIF format. The provided URI must be publicly accessible and at most 2 kB in length. The URI itself is saved with the image, and exposed via the ImageProperties.content_uri field.

tab_id.title: Tab Id
tab_id.description: The tab that the image to be replaced is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

image_object_id.title: Image Object Id
image_object_id.description: The ID of the existing image that will be replaced. The ID can be retrieved from the response of a get request.

image_replace_method.title: ImageReplaceMethod
image_replace_method.description: The replacement method. Defaults to CENTER_CROP if not specified.

replace_image.description: The details of the image replacement request.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies from the batchUpdate request. For a successful image replacement, this will typically be an empty list or contain a response related to the replaceImage request itself.

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The ID of the document in which the image was replaced.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows users to replace an existing image in a document with a new image specified by a URI. It ensures the document remains up-to-date with the latest visuals.

name: Search Developer Metadata
description: Tool to search for developer metadata in a spreadsheet. use when you need to find specific metadata entries based on filters.

a1Range.title: A1 Range
a1Range.description: Selects DeveloperMetadata associated with the given A1 range. Must represent a single row or single column only. Valid examples: 'A:A' (entire column A), 'Sheet1!B:B' (column B in Sheet1), '1:1' (entire row 1), 'Sheet1!5:5' (row 5 in Sheet1). Invalid examples: 'A1:D7' (multi-row/multi-column range), 'A1' (single cell).

gridRange.title: Grid Range
gridRange.description: Selects DeveloperMetadata associated with the given grid range. The developer metadata must be associated with a location that overlaps the range.

dataFilters.title: Data Filters
dataFilters.description: The data filters describing the criteria used to determine which DeveloperMetadata entries to return.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to retrieve metadata from.

a1Range.title: A1 Range
a1Range.description: Selects DeveloperMetadata associated with the given A1 range. Must represent a single row or single column only. Valid examples: 'A:A' (entire column A), 'Sheet1!B:B' (column B in Sheet1), '1:1' (entire row 1), 'Sheet1!5:5' (row 5 in Sheet1). Invalid examples: 'A1:D7' (multi-row/multi-column range), 'A1' (single cell).

gridRange.title: Grid Range
gridRange.description: Selects DeveloperMetadata associated with the given grid range. The developer metadata must be associated with a location that overlaps the range.

dataFilters.title: Data Filters

name: Search Documents
description: Search for google documents using various filters including name, content, date ranges, and more.

query.title: Query
query.description: Search query to filter documents. Can search by name (name contains 'report'), full text content (fullText contains 'important'), or use complex queries with operators like 'and', 'or', 'not'. Leave empty to get all documents.

order_by.title: Order By
order_by.description: Order results by field. Common options: 'modifiedTime desc', 'modifiedTime asc', 'name', 'createdTime desc'

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of documents to return (1-1000). Defaults to 10.

starred_only.title: Starred Only
starred_only.description: Whether to return only starred documents. Defaults to False.

created_after.title: Created After
created_after.description: Return documents created after this date. Use RFC 3339 format like '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'.

modified_after.title: Modified After
modified_after.description: Return documents modified after this date. Use RFC 3339 format like '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'.

shared_with_me.title: Shared With Me
shared_with_me.description: Whether to return only documents shared with the current user. Defaults to False.

include_trashed.title: Include Trashed
include_trashed.description: Whether to include documents in trash. Defaults to False.

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: List of Google Documents matching the search criteria. Each document contains id, name, modifiedTime, createdTime, webViewLink, and other metadata.

total_found.title: Total Found
total_found.description: Total number of documents found matching the search criteria.

next_page_token.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results, if more results are available.

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Search People
description: Searches contacts by matching the query against names, nicknames, emails, phone numbers, and organizations, optionally including 'other contacts'.

query.title: Query

name: Send Draft
description: Sends the specified, existing draft to the recipients in the to, cc, and bcc headers.

user_id.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

draft_id.title: Draft Id
draft_id.description: The ID of the draft to send.

id.description: The immutable ID of the sent message.

name: Send Email
description: Sends an email via gmail api using the authenticated user's google profile display name, requiring `is html=true` if the body contains html and valid `s3key`, `mimetype`, `name` for any attachment.

user_id.description: User's email address; the literal 'me' refers to the authenticated user.

It requires at least one recipient and either a subject or body for the email to be sent. name: List Settings
description: Returns all user settings for the authenticated user.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: Token specifying which result page to return.

syncToken.title: Sync Token
syncToken.description: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of results from the previous list request. It makes the result of this list request contain only entries that have changed since then. If the syncToken expires, the server will respond with a 410 GONE response code and the client should clear its storage and perform a full synchronization without any syncToken.

maxResults.title: Max Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. By default the value is 100 entries. The page size can never be larger than 250 entries.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the collection ("calendar#settings").

id.title: Id
id.description: The id of the user setting.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource ("calendar#setting").

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the user setting. The format of the value depends on the ID of the setting. It must always be a UTF-8 string of length up to 1024 characters.

nextPageToken.title: Next Page Token
nextPageToken.description: Token used to access the next page of this result. Omitted if no further results are available, in which case nextSyncToken is provided.

nextSyncToken.title: Next Sync Token
nextSyncToken.description: Token used at a later point in time to retrieve only the entries that have changed since this result was returned. Omitted if further results are available, in which case nextPageToken is provided.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

id.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

This action sets up a notification channel to watch for changes

name: Create sheet from JSON
description: Creates a new google spreadsheet and populates its first worksheet from `sheet json`, which must be non-empty as its first item's keys establish the headers.

title.title: Title
title.description: The desired title for the new Google Spreadsheet.

sheet_json.title: Sheet Json
sheet_json.description: A list of dictionaries representing the rows of the sheet. Each dictionary must have the same set of keys, which will form the header row. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or null (represented as empty cells).

sheet_name.title: Sheet Name
sheet_name.description: The name for the first worksheet within the newly created spreadsheet. This name will appear as a tab at the bottom of the sheet.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: A dictionary containing the full response from the Google Sheets API after creating and populating the sheet. This typically includes details such as the spreadsheet ID, and information about the range of cells updated.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action creates a new Google Spreadsheet and fills its first worksheet with data provided in JSON format. It requires a title for the spreadsheet, a name for the sheet, and a list of data rows. An empty list is allowed to create an empty spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to copy.

index.title: Index
index.description: The index of the sheet, zero-based.

hidden.title: Hidden

tab_color.title: Tab Color
tab_color.description: The color of the tab in the UI, as a RGB color. Deprecated: Use tabColorStyle.

right_to_left.title: Right To Left
right_to_left.description: True if the sheet is an RTL sheet, false otherwise.

row_count.title: Row Count
row_count.description: The number of rows in the sheet.

column_count.title: Column Count

hide_gridlines.title: Hide Gridlines
hide_gridlines.description: True if gridlines are hidden, false otherwise.

row_group_control_after.title: Row Group Control After

human_description: Copies a single sheet from one Google Spreadsheet to another. Use this when you need to duplicate a sheet's data and formatting into a different spreadsheet.

name: Append Values to Spreadsheet
description: Tool to append values to a spreadsheet. use when you need to add new data to the end of an existing table in a google sheet.

range.title: Range
range.description: The A1 notation of a range to search for a logical table of data. Values are appended after the last row of the table.

values.title: Values
values.description: The data to be written. This is an array of arrays, the outer array representing all the data and each inner array representing a major dimension. Each item in the inner array corresponds with one cell.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

majorDimension.title: Major Dimension
majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values. For output, if the spreadsheet data is: A1=1,B1=2,A2=3,B2=4, then requesting range=A1:B2,majorDimension=ROWS will return [[1,2],[3,4]], whereas requesting range=A1:B2,majorDimension=COLUMNS will return [[1,3],[2,4]].

insertDataOption.title: Insert Data Option
insertDataOption.description: How the input data should be inserted.

valueInputOption.title: Value Input Option
valueInputOption.description: How the input data should be interpreted.

includeValuesInResponse.title: Include Values In Response
includeValuesInResponse.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were appended. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

responseValueRenderOption.title: Response Value Render Option
responseValueRenderOption.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE.

responseDateTimeRenderOption.title: Response Date Time Render Option
responseDateTimeRenderOption.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. This is ignored if responseValueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER.

updates.title: UpdateValuesResponse

updatedData.title: ValueRange

range.title: Range
range.description: The range the values cover, in A1 notation. For output, this range indicates the entire requested range, even though the values will exclude trailing rows and columns. When appending values, this field represents the range to search for a table, after which values will be appended.

values.title: Values
values.description: The data that was read or to be written. This is an array of arrays, the outer array representing all the data and each inner array representing a major dimension. Each item in the inner array corresponds with one cell.

majorDimension.title: Major Dimension
majorDimension.description: The major dimension of the values. For output, if the spreadsheet data is: `A1=1,B1=2,A2=3,B2=4`, then requesting `range=A1:B2,majorDimension=ROWS` will return `[[1,2],[3,4]]`, whereas requesting `range=A1:B2,majorDimension=COLUMNS` will return `[[1,3],[2,4]]`.

updatedData.description: The values of the cells that were updated. Only fill in if the request's includeValuesInResponse field was true.

updatedRange.title: Updated Range
updatedRange.description: The range (in A1 notation) that updates were applied to.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The spreadsheet the updates were applied to.

updates.description: Information about the updates that were applied.

tableRange.title: Table Range
tableRange.description: The range (in A1 notation) of the table that values are being appended to (before the values were appended). Empty if no table was found.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The spreadsheet the updates were applied to.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action appends values to the specified range in a Google Sheets spreadsheet. It allows users to add new data seamlessly to existing tables.

name: Sync Events
description: Synchronizes google calendar events, performing a full sync if no `sync token` is provided or if a 410 gone error (due to an expired token) necessitates it, otherwise performs an incremental sync for events changed since the `sync token` was issued.

pageToken.title: Page Token
pageToken.description: Token for paginating results, from a previous response's `nextPageToken`.

sync_token.title: Sync Token
sync_token.description: Token for incremental sync, retrieving only changes since issued. A 410 GONE response indicates an expired token, requiring a full sync.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Google Calendar identifier; 'primary' refers to the authenticated user's main calendar.

event_types.title: Event Types
event_types.description: Filters events by specified types (e.g., 'default', 'focusTime', 'outOfOffice', 'workingLocation'). All types returned if omitted.

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Max events per page (max 2500); Google Calendar's default is used if unspecified.

single_events.title: Single Events
single_events.description: If True, expands recurring events into individual instances (excluding master event); otherwise, Google's default handling applies.

event_data.title: Event Data
event_data.description: Dictionary with synchronized data: 'event_data' (list of changed event resources), 'nextPageToken' (for pagination), and 'nextSyncToken' (for next incremental sync).

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Synchronizes events from Google Calendar based on the provided parameters.
    It can perform either a full sync or incremental sync depending on the conditions of the sync token.

name: Update ACL Rule
description: Updates an access control rule for the specified calendar.

role.title: Role
role.description:
The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: 
- "none" - Provides no access. 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role with the additional ability to see and manipulate ACLs.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

send_notifications.title: Send Notifications
send_notifications.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource ("calendar#aclRule").

role.title: Role
role.description:
The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: 
- "none" - Provides no access. 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role with the additional ability to see and manipulate ACLs.

type.title: Type
type.description:
The type of the scope. Possible values are: 
- "default" - The public scope. This is the default value. 
- "user" - Limits the scope to a single user. 
- "group" - Limits the scope to a group. 
- "domain" - Limits the scope to a domain.  Note: This requires ownership of the domain.

value.title: Value
value.description: The email address or domain name in the scope. If left blank, the scope is of type "default", meaning it is public. Optional.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action modifies the access permissions for a specified rule on a calendar. It allows you to change the role assigned to a user or group, affecting their access level to the calendar.

content.title: Content
examples: This is the updated comment content.
content.description: The plain text content of the comment. This field is used to update the comment's text. If not provided, the existing content will be retained unless 'resolved' is being updated.

resolved.title: Resolved

name: Update Document Markdown
description: Replaces the entire content of an existing google docs document with new markdown text; requires edit permissions for the document.

input_parameters.title: UpdateDocumentMarkdownRequest

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: Identifier of the Google Docs document to update, found in the document's URL.

new_markdown_text.title: New Markdown Text
examples:
# Document Title

This is a paragraph with **bold** and *italic* text.

- Unordered list item 1
- Unordered list item 2

1. Ordered list item 1
2. Ordered list item 2

An image: ![Alt text for image](https://www.example.com/image.png)

## Advanced Markdown


# Example Python code
def greet(name):
    print(f'Hello, {name}!')
greet('World')


| Column A | Column B |
|----------|----------|
|   Data 1 |   Data 2 |
|   Data 3 |   Data 4 |

> This is a blockquote.
new_markdown_text.description: Markdown content that will replace the document's entire existing content. Supports standard Markdown features.

input_parameters.description: Defines the request schema for updating an existing Google Docs document by replacing its content with new text formatted in Markdown.

data.title: Data
data.description: A dictionary containing the raw response from the Google Docs API's `batchUpdate` endpoint. This can include details about the revisions made and the document's current state. Its structure is defined by the Google Docs API.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Docs document that was successfully updated.

human_description: This action updates a Google Docs document by replacing its content with new text in Markdown format. It requires edit permissions and transforms the provided Markdown into the appropriate document format.

name: Update Document Style
description: Tool to update the overall document style, such as page size, margins, and default text direction. use when you need to modify the global style settings of a google document.

successful.title: Successful

canChangeSharingFoldersRequiresOrganizer.title: Can Change Sharing Folders Requires Organizer Permission Restriction

capabilities.description: Capabilities the current user has on this shared drive.

restrictions.title: DriveRestrictions

domainUsersOnly.title: Domain Users Only
domainUsersOnly.description: If true, restricts access to users of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

driveMembersOnly.title: Drive Members Only
driveMembersOnly.description: If true, restricts access to items inside the shared drive to its members.

adminManagedRestrictions.title: Admin Managed Restrictions
adminManagedRestrictions.description: If true, requires administrative privileges to modify restrictions.

copyRequiresWriterPermission.title: Copy Requires Writer Permission
copyRequiresWriterPermission.description: If true, disables copy, print, or download options for readers and commenters.

sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermissio.title: Sharing Folders Requires Organizer Permission
sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermissio.description: If true, only users with the organizer role can share folders. If false, users with either the organizer or file organizer role can share folders.

restrictions.description: A set of restrictions that apply to this shared drive.

backgroundImageFile.title: BackgroundImageFileResponse
backgroundImageFile.description: The background image file of this shared drive.

backgroundImageLink.title: Background Image Link
backgroundImageLink.description: A short-lived link to this shared drive's background image.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the metadata of a specified shared drive in Google Drive. It allows you to change the drive's name, color, theme, background image, and apply certain restrictions.

name: Update Google event
description: Updates an existing event by `event id` in a google calendar; this is a full put replacement, so provide all desired fields as unspecified ones may be cleared or reset.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: The unique identifier of the event to be updated.

location.title: Location
location.description: Geographic location of the event as free-form text.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone name (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Required if datetime is naive. If datetime includes timezone info (Z or offset), this field is optional and defaults to UTC.

eventType.title: Event Type
eventType.description: Type of the event, immutable post-creation. Supported types: 'birthday' (all-day with annual recurrence), 'default' (regular event), 'focusTime' (focus-time event), 'outOfOffice' (out-of-office event), 'workingLocation' (working location event). Note: 'fromGmail' events cannot be created via API.

recurrence.title: Recurrence
recurrence.description: List of RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE, EXDATE lines for recurring events. Supported frequencies are DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY.

calendar_id.title: Calendar Id
calendar_id.description: Identifier of the Google Calendar where the event resides. The value 'primary' targets the user's primary calendar.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the event. Can contain HTML. Optional.

send_updates.title: Send Updates
send_updates.description: Defaults to True. Whether to send updates to the attendees.

start_datetime.title: Start Datetime
start_datetime.description: Naive date/time (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) with NO offsets or Z. e.g. '2025-01-16T13:00:00'

guests_can_modify.title: Guests Can Modify
guests_can_modify.description: If True, guests can modify the event.

create_meeting_room.title: Create Meeting Room
create_meeting_room.description: If true, a Google Meet link is created and added to the event. CRITICAL: As of 2024, this REQUIRES a paid Google Workspace account ($13+/month). Personal Gmail accounts will fail with 'Invalid conference type value' error. Solutions: 1) Upgrade to Workspace, 2) Use domain-wide delegation with Workspace user, 3) Use the new Google Meet REST API, or 4) Create events without conferences. See https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-nodejs-client/issues/3234

event_duration_hour.title: Event Duration Hour
event_duration_hour.description: Number of hours (0-24). Increase by 1 here rather than passing 60 in `event_duration_minutes`

guestsCanInviteOthers.title: Guests Can Invite Others
guestsCanInviteOthers.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event.

event_duration_minutes.title: Event Duration Minutes
event_duration_minutes.description: Duration in minutes (0-59 ONLY). NEVER use 60+ minutes - use event_duration_hour=1 instead. Maximum value is 59.

guestsCanSeeOtherGuests.title: Guests Can See Other Guests
guestsCanSeeOtherGuests.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are.

workingLocationProperties.title: Working Location Properties
workingLocationProperties.description: Working location properties for workingLocation events. Must include 'type' field with value: 'homeOffice', 'officeLocation', or 'customLocation'.

response_data.title: Response Data
response_data.description: Complete representation of the updated event resource from the Google Calendar API.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Updates an existing event in Google Calendar. This action replaces the event with the provided details, allowing changes to the title, time, attendees, location, and other event properties.

name: Update existing document
description: Applies programmatic edits, such as text insertion, deletion, or formatting, to a specified google doc using the `batchupdate` api method.

input_parameters.title: DocumentRequest

editDocs.title: Edit Docs

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Docs document to be updated.

input_parameters.description: Request model for updating an existing Google Docs document with specified edit operations.

data.title: Data
data.description: The full response data from the Google Docs API `batchUpdate` request, containing details about the applied edits and the state of the document.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Docs document that was updated.

human_description: This action updates a Google Docs document by applying various edit operations. You can insert, delete, or format text as specified in the request body.

name: Update File (Metadata)
description: Updates file metadata. uses patch semantics (partial update) as per google drive api v3. use this tool to modify attributes of an existing file like its name, description, or parent folders. note: this action currently supports metadata updates only. file content updates require multipart/related upload and are not yet implemented.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: The ID of the file to update.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive will attempt to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content if no value is provided. The value cannot be changed unless a new revision is uploaded.

add_parents.title: Add Parents
add_parents.description: A comma-separated list of parent IDs to add.

description.title: Description
examples: Updated version of the project proposal.
description.description: A short description of the file.

ocr_language.title: Ocr Language
ocr_language.description: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

remove_parents.title: Remove Parents
remove_parents.description: A comma-separated list of parent IDs to remove.

writers_can_share.title: Writers Can Share
writers_can_share.description: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

supports_all_drives.title: Supports All Drives
supports_all_drives.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. This parameter will always be true by default if the application is configured to support shared drives.

keep_revision_forever.title: Keep Revision Forever
keep_revision_forever.description: Whether to set this revision of the file to be kept forever. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.

use_domain_admin_access.title: Use Domain Admin Access
use_domain_admin_access.description: Whether the requesting application is using domain-wide delegation to access content belonging to a user in a different domain. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

viewers_can_copy_content.title: Viewers Can Copy Content
viewers_can_copy_content.description: Whether viewers are prevented from copying content of the file.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#file'.

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the file's content in bytes. This is applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive and Google Docs files.

owners.title: Owners
owners.description: The owners of the file. Currently, only certain legacy files may have more than one owner.

shared.title: Shared
shared.description: Whether the file has been shared.

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: The list of spaces which contain the file. The currently supported values are 'drive', 'appDataFolder' and 'photos'.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

trashed.title: Trashed
trashed.description: Whether the file has been trashed, either explicitly or from a trashed parent folder.

version.title: Version
version.description: A monotonically increasing version number for the file. This reflects every change made to the file on the server, even those not visible to the user.

drive_id.title: Drive Id
drive_id.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in. This is an alias for teamDriveId.

icon_link.title: Icon Link
icon_link.description: A static, unauthenticated link to the file's icon.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: The MIME type of the file.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are private to the requesting app.

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the file.

owned_by_me.title: Owned By Me
owned_by_me.description: Whether the user owns the file.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The full list of permissions for the file. This is only available if the requesting user has sufficient permission to see the full list of permissions.

capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.description: Capabilities the current user has on this file. Each capability corresponds to a fine-grained permission that a user may have.

created_time.title: Created Time
created_time.description: The time at which the file was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

export_links.title: Export Links
export_links.description: Links for exporting Google Docs to specific formats.

md5_checksum.title: Md5 Checksum
md5_checksum.description: The MD5 checksum for the content of the file. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

resource_key.title: Resource Key
resource_key.description: A key needed to access the item via a shared link.

sharing_user.title: Sharing User
sharing_user.description: The user who shared the file with the requesting user, if applicable.

viewed_by_me.title: Viewed By Me
viewed_by_me.description: Whether the file has been viewed by this user.

content_hints.title: Content Hints
content_hints.description: Additional information about the content of the file. These fields are never populated in responses.

has_thumbnail.title: Has Thumbnail
has_thumbnail.description: Whether this file has a thumbnail.

modified_time.title: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The last time the file was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time).

team_drive_id.title: Team Drive Id
team_drive_id.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

web_view_link.title: Web View Link
web_view_link.description: A link for opening the file in a relevant Google editor or viewer in a browser.

app_properties.title: App Properties
app_properties.description: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are private to the requesting app.

file_extension.title: File Extension
file_extension.description: The final component of fullFileExtension. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

permission_ids.title: Permission Ids
permission_ids.description: A list of permission IDs for users with access to this file.

thumbnail_link.title: Thumbnail Link
thumbnail_link.description: A short-lived link to the file's thumbnail. Typically lasts on the order of hours. Only populated when the requesting app can access the file's content.

head_revision_id.title: Head Revision Id
head_revision_id.description: The ID of the head revision of the file.

quota_bytes_used.title: Quota Bytes Used
quota_bytes_used.description: The number of storage quota bytes used by the file. This includes the head revision as well as previous revisions with keepForever enabled.

shortcut_details.title: Shortcut Details
shortcut_details.description: Shortcut file details. Only populated for shortcut files, which have the mimeType field set to application/vnd.google-apps.shortcut.

web_content_link.title: Web Content Link
web_content_link.description: A link for downloading the content of the file in a browser. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

is_app_authorized.title: Is App Authorized
is_app_authorized.description: Whether the file was created or opened by the requesting app.

original_filename.title: Original Filename
original_filename.description: The original filename of the uploaded content if available, or else the original value of the name field. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

viewed_by_me_time.title: Viewed By Me Time
viewed_by_me_time.description: The last time the file was viewed by the user (RFC 3339 date-time).

writers_can_share.title: Writers Can Share
writers_can_share.description: Whether writers can share the document with other users. This field is now deprecated: users should use the capabilities field on a file. The capabilities field can tell you if a user can share a file.

full_file_extension.title: Full File Extension
full_file_extension.description: The full file extension extracted from the name field. May contain multiple concatenated extensions, suchas "tar.gz". This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

last_modifying_user.title: Last Modifying User
last_modifying_user.description: The last user to modify the file.

link_share_metadata.title: Link Share Metadata
link_share_metadata.description: Metadata about the shared link. This will only be present for files with link-sharing enabled.

modified_by_me_time.title: Modified By Me Time
modified_by_me_time.description: The last time the file was modified by the user (RFC 3339 date-time).

shared_with_me_time.title: Shared With Me Time
shared_with_me_time.description: The time at which the file was shared with the user, if applicable (RFC 3339 date-time).

image_media_metadata.title: Image Media Metadata
image_media_metadata.description: Metadata about image media. This will only be present for image types, and its contents will depend on the image type.

video_media_metadata.title: Video Media Metadata
video_media_metadata.description: Metadata about video media. This will only be present for video types.

viewers_can_copy_content.title: Viewers Can Copy Content
viewers_can_copy_content.description: Whether viewers are prevented from copying content of the file made available for offline access.

copy_requires_writer_permission.title: Copy Requires Writer Permission
copy_requires_writer_permission.description: Whether users with only writer permission can modify the file's permissions. This field is now deprecated: use writersCanShare instead.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action allows you to update the metadata of an existing file in Google Drive. You can modify attributes such as the file's name, description, and parent folder associations.

name: Update File Revision Metadata
description: Updates metadata of a file revision (e.g., keepforever, publish). use this tool to modify the metadata of a specific revision of a file in google drive.

published.title: Published
published.description: Whether this revision is published. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

publishAuto.title: Publish Auto
publishAuto.description: Whether subsequent revisions will be automatically republished. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

keep_forever.title: Keep Forever
keep_forever.description: Whether to keep this revision forever, even if it is no longer the head revision. If not set, the revision will be automatically purged 30 days after newer content is uploaded. This can be set on a maximum of 200 revisions for a file. This field is only applicable to files with binary content in Drive.

publishedOutsideDomain.title: Published Outside Domain
publishedOutsideDomain.description: Whether this revision is published outside the domain. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#revision".

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the revision's content in bytes.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the revision.

published.title: Published
published.description: Whether this revision is published. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

exportLinks.title: Export Links
exportLinks.description: Links for exporting Docs Editors files to specific formats.

keepForever.title: Keep Forever
keepForever.description: Whether to keep this revision forever.

md5Checksum.title: Md5 Checksum
md5Checksum.description: The MD5 checksum of the revision's content.

publishAuto.title: Publish Auto
publishAuto.description: Whether subsequent revisions will be automatically republished.

modifiedTime.title: Modified Time
modifiedTime.description: The last time the revision was modified (RFC 3339 date-time).

publishedLink.title: Published Link
publishedLink.description: A link to the published revision.

originalFilename.title: Original Filename
originalFilename.description: The original filename used to create this revision.

lastModifyingUser.title: User

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string "drive#user".

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: A plain text displayable name for this user.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user. This may not be present in certain contexts if the user has not made their email address visible to the requester.

permissionId.title: Permission Id
permissionId.description: The user's ID as visible in Permission resources.

lastModifyingUser.description: The last user to modify this revision.

publishedOutsideDomain.title: Published Outside Domain
publishedOutsideDomain.description: Whether this revision is published outside the domain.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description:
Updates metadata properties of a specific file revision in Google Drive (NOT file content).
    Use this to mark a revision as 'keep forever' or to publish/unpublish a revision.
    Requires both file_id and revision_id. Does NOT support updating file content - use Edit File for that.

name: Update Permission
description: Tool to update a permission with patch semantics. use when you need to modify an existing permission for a file or shared drive.

permission.title: Permission

expirationTime.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time).

permissionId.description: The ID of the permission.

removeExpiration.description: Whether to remove the expiration date.

transferOwnership.title: Transfer Ownership
transferOwnership.description: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user and downgrade the current owner to a writer. This parameter is required as an acknowledgement of the side effect when set to true.

useDomainAdminAccess.title: Use Domain Admin Access
useDomainAdminAccess.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

enforceExpansiveAccess.title: Enforce Expansive Access
enforceExpansiveAccess.description: Whether the request should enforce expansive access rules. This field is deprecated, it is recommended to use `permissionDetails` instead.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies what kind of resource this is. Value: the fixed string 'drive#permission'.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role granted by this permission.

type.title: Type

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain to which this permission refers.

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Whether the permission has been deleted. This is always false for permissions that haven't been deleted.

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user or group to which this permission refers.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user or group to which this permission refers.

pendingOwner.title: Pending Owner
pendingOwner.description: Whether the account is pending owner. Output only.

expirationTime.title: Expiration Time
expirationTime.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time).

permissionDetails.title: Permission Details
permissionDetails.description: Details of whether the permissions on this shared drive item are inherited or directly on this item. This is an output-only field which is present only for shared drive items.

successful.title: Successful

updateMask.description: Optional. Field mask used to specify the fields to be updated. Comma-separated list of fully qualified field names. Example: "config.accessType,config.moderationRestrictions.chatRestriction". Use "*" to update all fields.

config.title: SpaceConfig

accessType.title: AccessType

moderation.title: ModerationMode
moderation.description: The pre-configured moderation mode for the Meeting.

autoSmartNotesGeneration.title: AutoGenerationType
autoSmartNotesGeneration.description: Defines whether to automatically generate a summary and recap of the meeting.

autoTranscriptionGeneration.title: AutoGenerationType
autoTranscriptionGeneration.description: Defines whether the content of a meeting is automatically transcribed.

artifactConfig.description: Configuration pertaining to the auto-generated artifacts.

entryPointAccess.title: EntryPointAccess
entryPointAccess.description: Defines the entry points that can be used to join meetings.

moderationRestrictions.title: ModerationRestrictions

chatRestriction.title: RestrictionType
chatRestriction.description: Defines who has permission to send chat messages.

presentRestriction.title: RestrictionType

reactionRestriction.title: RestrictionType

defaultJoinAsViewerType.title: DefaultJoinAsViewerType
defaultJoinAsViewerType.description: Defines whether to restrict the default role assigned to users as viewer.

moderationRestrictions.description: When moderation.ON, these restrictions go into effect.

attendanceReportGenerationType.title: AttendanceReportGenerationType
attendanceReportGenerationType.description: Whether attendance report is enabled for the meeting space.

config.description: Configuration pertaining to the meeting space.

meetingCode.title: Meeting Code
meetingCode.description: Type friendly unique string used to join the meeting.

error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful

name: Update Spreadsheet Properties
description: Tool to update properties of a spreadsheet, such as its title, locale, or auto-recalculation settings. use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a google sheet.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: The fields that should be updated. Use '*' for all fields in 'properties' or a comma-separated list (e.g., 'title,locale,iterativeCalculationSettings.maxIterations'). The root 'properties' is implied.

properties.title: Properties

title.title: Title
title.description: The title of the spreadsheet.

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: The locale of the spreadsheet in BCP 47 format.

timeZone.title: Time Zone
timeZone.description: The time zone of the spreadsheet in CLDR format (e.g., 'America/New_York').

autoRecalc.title: AutoRecalcEnum
autoRecalc.description: The recalculation interval for the spreadsheet.

defaultFormat.title: CellFormat

textFormat.title: Text Format
textFormat.description: The text format of the cell. Represents a TextFormat object.

wrapStrategy.title: Wrap Strategy
wrapStrategy.description: The wrap strategy of the cell content. E.g., 'OVERFLOW_CELL', 'LEGACY_WRAP', 'CLIP', 'WRAP'.

verticalAlignment.title: Vertical Alignment
verticalAlignment.description: The vertical alignment of the cell content. E.g., 'TOP', 'MIDDLE', 'BOTTOM'.

horizontalAlignment.title: Horizontal Alignment
horizontalAlignment.description: The horizontal alignment of the cell content. E.g., 'LEFT', 'CENTER', 'RIGHT'.

backgroundColorStyle.title: Background Color Style
backgroundColorStyle.description: The background color style of the cell. Represents a ColorStyle object.

defaultFormat.description: The default cell format for the entire spreadsheet.

themeColors.title: Theme Colors
themeColors.description: The theme colors of the spreadsheet. This is a list of ThemeColorPair.

primaryFontFamily.title: Primary Font Family
primaryFontFamily.description: The primary font family of the spreadsheet theme.

maxIterations.title: Max Iterations
maxIterations.description: The maximum number of iterations for iterative calculation.

convergenceThreshold.title: Convergence Threshold
convergenceThreshold.description: The threshold for convergence in iterative calculation.

properties.description: The properties to update. At least one field within properties must be set.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

replies.title: Replies
replies.description: A list of replies to the batch update requests. For this action, it will contain one reply corresponding to the updateSpreadsheetProperties request.

spreadsheetId.title: Spreadsheet Id
spreadsheetId.description: The ID of the updated spreadsheet.

updatedSpreadsheet.title: Updated Spreadsheet
updatedSpreadsheet.description: The spreadsheet resource with the updated properties.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: Updates the overall SPREADSHEET-LEVEL properties of a Google Spreadsheet like its title, locale, time zone, and auto-recalculation settings. Use this to modify the spreadsheet's configuration. For updating individual sheet/tab properties (like renaming a sheet), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.

name: Update Table Row Style
description: Tool to update the style of a table row in a google document. use when you need to modify the appearance of specific rows within a table, such as setting minimum row height or marking rows as headers.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document to update.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: The fields that should be updated. At least one field must be specified. The root tableRowStyle is implied and should not be specified. A single "*" can be used as short-hand for listing every field. For example to update the minimum row height, set fields to "minRowHeight".

rowIndices.title: Row Indices
rowIndices.description: The list of zero-based row indices whose style should be updated. If no indices are specified, all rows will be updated.

tableRowStyle.title: Table Row Style

tableHeader.title: Table Header
tableHeader.description: Whether the row is a table header.

minRowHeight.title: Dimension
minRowHeight.description: The minimum height of the row. The row will be rendered in the Docs editor at a height equal to or greater than this value in order to show all the content in the row's cells.

preventOverflow.title: Prevent Overflow
preventOverflow.description: Whether the row cannot overflow across page or column boundaries.

tableRowStyle.description: The styles to be set on the rows.

tableStartLocation.title: Table Start Location

index.title: Index
index.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units.

tabId.title: Tab Id
tabId.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

segmentId.title: Segment Id
segmentId.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

tableStartLocation.description: The location where the table starts in the document.

reply.title: Reply
reply.description: A map of responses to the batchUpdate request, keyed by request ID.

documentId.title: Document Id
documentId.description: The ID of the document.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

human_description: This action updates the style of specified rows in a Google Document's table. It allows users to set attributes like minimum row height, table header status, and overflow prevention for a better document presentation.

name: Update Task
description: Updates the specified task.

due.title: Due
due.description: Due date of the task (RFC 3339 timestamp).

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task.

due.title: Due
due.description: Due date of the task (as a RFC 3339 timestamp).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource. This is always "tasks#task".

type.title: Type
type.description: Type of the link.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the link.

links.title: Links
links.description: Collection of links. This collection is read-only.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Notes describing the task. Maximum length allowed: 8192 characters.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

hidden.title: Hidden
hidden.description: Flag indicating whether the task is hidden. The default is False.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Parent task identifier. This field is omitted if it is a top-level task.

status.title: Status
status.description: Status of the task. This is either "needsAction" or "completed".

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Flag indicating whether the task has been deleted. The default is False.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Last modification time of the task (as a RFC 3339 timestamp).

position.title: Position
position.description: String indicating the position of the task among its sibling tasks under the same parent task or at the top level. If the parent task is specified then the task is inserted at the top of the subtasks list (before the first subtask). If the task is a top-level task then the task is inserted at the top of the top-level tasks list (before the first top-level task).

selfLink.title: Self Link
selfLink.description: URL pointing to this task. Used to retrieve, update, or delete this task.

completed.title: Completed
completed.description: Completion date of the task (as a RFC 3339 timestamp). This field is omitted if the task has not been completed.

webViewLink.title: Web View Link
webViewLink.description: An absolute link to the task in the Google Tasks Web UI.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Update Task List
description: Updates the authenticated user's specified task list.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task list. Maximum length allowed: 1024 characters.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Type of the resource. This is always "tasks#taskList".

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the task list.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Last modification time of the task list (as a RFC 3339 timestamp).

selfLink.title: Self Link
selfLink.description: URL pointing to this task list. Used to retrieve, update, or delete this task list.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
successful.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Upload File
description: Uploads a file (max 5mb) to google drive, moving it to a specified folder if a valid folder id is provided, otherwise uploads to root.

file_to_upload.title: FileUploadable
file_to_upload.description: File to upload to Google Drive (max 5MB), identified by its name or path.

folder_to_upload_to.title: Folder To Upload To
folder_to_upload_to.description: Optional ID of the target Google Drive folder; can be obtained using 'Find Folder' or similar actions.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource type. This is always 'drive#file'.

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the file's content in bytes. This is applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive and Google Docs files.

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource type (e.g., drive#user).

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user.

permissionId.title: Permission Id
permissionId.description: The ETag of the permission.

owners.title: Owners
owners.description: The owners of the file. Currently, only certain legacy files may have more than one owner.

shared.title: Shared
shared.description: Whether the file has been shared.

spaces.title: Spaces
spaces.description: The list of spaces which contain the file. The currently supported values are 'drive', 'appDataFolder' and 'photos'.

driveId.title: Drive Id
driveId.description: The ID of the shared drive the file resides in. Only populated for items in shared drives.

parents.title: Parents
parents.description: The IDs of the parent folders which contain the file.

starred.title: Starred
starred.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

trashed.title: Trashed
trashed.description: Whether the file has been trashed, either explicitly or from a trashed parent folder.

version.title: Version
version.description: A monotonically increasing version number for the file. This reflects every change made to the file on the server, even those not visible to the user.

iconLink.title: Icon Link
iconLink.description: A static, unauthenticated link to the file's icon.

mimeType.title: Mime Type
mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

labelInfo.title: LabelInfo
labelInfo.description: Information about labels applied to the file.

ownedByMe.title: Owned By Me
ownedByMe.description: Whether the user owns the file.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are visible to all apps.

viewedByMe.title: Viewed By Me
viewedByMe.description: Whether the file has been viewed by this user.

createdTime.title: Created Time
createdTime.description: The time at which the file was created (RFC 3339 date-time).

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the file.

exportLinks.title: Export Links
exportLinks.description: Links for exporting Google Docs to specific formats.

md5Checksum.title: Md5 Checksum
md5Checksum.description: The MD5 checksum for the content of the file. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The permissions for the file. This field is only populated for items in shared drives.

resourceKey.title: Resource Key
resourceKey.description: A key needed to access the item via a shared link.

sharingUser.title: User

me.title: Me
me.description: Whether this user is the requesting user.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Identifies the resource type (e.g., drive#user).

photoLink.title: Photo Link
photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: The display name of the user.

emailAddress.title: Email Address
emailAddress.description: The email address of the user.

permissionId.title: Permission Id
permissionId.description: The ETag of the permission.

sharingUser.description: The user who shared the file with the current user, if applicable.

webViewLink.title: Web View Link
webViewLink.description: A link for opening the file in a relevant Google editor or viewer in a browser.

capabilities.title: Capabilities

canMoveItemOutOfDrive.title: Can Move Item Out Of Drive

mimeType.title: Mime Type

indexableText.title: Indexable Text
indexableText.description: Text to be indexed for the file to improve searchability.

contentHints.description: Additional information about the content of the file. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

hasThumbnail.title: Has Thumbnail
hasThumbnail.description: Whether this file has a thumbnail.

modifiedByMe.title: Modified By Me
modifiedByMe.description: Whether the file has been modified by this user.

modifiedTime.title: Modified Time
modifiedTime.description: The last time the file was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time).

sha1Checksum.title: Sha1 Checksum
sha1Checksum.description: The SHA1 checksum for the content of the file. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

me.title: Me

kind.title: Kind

photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

permissionId.description: The ETag of the permission.

appProperties.title: App Properties
appProperties.description: A collection of arbitrary key-value pairs which are private to the requesting app.

fileExtension.title: File Extension
fileExtension.description: The final component of fullFileExtension. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

permissionIds.title: Permission Ids
permissionIds.description: A list of permission IDs for users with access to this file.

thumbnailLink.title: Thumbnail Link
thumbnailLink.description: A short-lived link to the file's thumbnail. Typically lasts on the order of hours. Only populated when the requesting app can access the file's content.

folderColorRgb.title: Folder Color Rgb
folderColorRgb.description: The color for a folder as an RGB hex string. E.g. #4f8df3

headRevisionId.title: Head Revision Id
headRevisionId.description: The ID of the head revision of the file.

quotaBytesUsed.title: Quota Bytes Used
quotaBytesUsed.description: The number of bytes the file occupies in storage. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

sha256Checksum.title: Sha256 Checksum
sha256Checksum.description: The SHA256 checksum for the content of the file. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

viewedByMeTime.title: Viewed By Me Time
viewedByMeTime.description: The last time the file was viewed by the user (RFC 3339 date-time).

webContentLink.title: Web Content Link
webContentLink.description: A link for downloading the content of the file in a browser. This is only available for files with binary content in Google Drive.

isAppAuthorized.title: Is App Authorized
isAppAuthorized.description: Whether the file was created or opened by the requesting app.

shortcutDetails.title: ShortcutDetails

targetId.description: The ID of the file that this shortcut points to.

targetMimeType.title: Target Mime Type
targetMimeType.description: The MIME type of the file that this shortcut points to.

targetResourceKey.title: Target Resource Key
targetResourceKey.description: The resource key of the target file.

shortcutDetails.description: Shortcut file details. Only populated for shortcut files, which have the mimeType field set to application/vnd.google-apps.shortcut.

writersCanShare.title: Writers Can Share
writersCanShare.description: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

modifiedByMeTime.title: Modified By Me Time
modifiedByMeTime.description: The last time the file was modified by the user (RFC 3339 date-time).

originalFilename.title: Original Filename
originalFilename.description: The original filename of the uploaded content if available, or else the original value of the name field. Once set, it will only change if a new revision is uploaded.

sharedWithMeTime.title: Shared With Me Time
sharedWithMeTime.description: The time at which the file was shared with the user (RFC 3339 date-time).

thumbnailVersion.title: Thumbnail Version
thumbnailVersion.description: The version of the file's thumbnail. Available when the media is hosted on Google Drive.

explicitlyTrashed.title: Explicitly Trashed
explicitlyTrashed.description: Whether the file has been explicitly trashed, as opposed to recursively trashed from a parent folder.

fullFileExtension.title: Full File Extension
fullFileExtension.description: The full file extension extracted from the name field. May contain multiple concatenated extensions, such as 'tar.gz'.

photoLink.description: A link to the user's profile photo, if available.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the user.

lastModifyingUser.description: The last user to modify the file.

linkShareMetadata.description: Metadata about the shared link for this file.

lens.title: Lens
lens.description: The lens used to create the image.

time.title: Time
time.description: The time the photo was taken (Exif DateTime).

width.title: Width
width.description: The width of the image in pixels.

height.title: Height
height.description: The height of the image in pixels.

sensor.title: Sensor
sensor.description: The sensor used to create the image.

aperture.title: Aperture
aperture.description: The aperture used to create the image.

isoSpeed.title: Iso Speed
isoSpeed.description: The ISO speed used to create the image.

location.title: Location
location.description: Geographic location information for the image.

rotation.title: Rotation
rotation.description: The rotation of the image in degrees.

flashUsed.title: Flash Used
flashUsed.description: Whether a flash was used to create the image.

cameraMake.title: Camera Make
cameraMake.description: The make of the camera used to create the image.

colorSpace.title: Color Space
colorSpace.description: The color space of the image.

cameraModel.title: Camera Model
cameraModel.description: The model of the camera used to create the image.

focalLength.title: Focal Length
focalLength.description: The focal length used to create the image.

exposureBias.title: Exposure Bias
exposureBias.description: The exposure bias of the image.

exposureMode.title: Exposure Mode
exposureMode.description: The exposure mode used to create the image.

exposureTime.title: Exposure Time
exposureTime.description: The exposure time used to create the image.

meteringMode.title: Metering Mode
meteringMode.description: The metering mode used to create the image.

whiteBalance.title: White Balance
whiteBalance.description: The white balance mode used to create the image.

subjectDistance.title: Subject Distance
subjectDistance.description: The subject distance of the image.

maxApertureValue.title: Max Aperture Value
maxApertureValue.description: The maximum aperture value of the image.

imageMediaMetadata.description: Additional metadata about image media, if available.

videoMediaMetadata.title: VideoMediaMetadata

width.title: Width
width.description: The width of the video in pixels.

height.title: Height
height.description: The height of the video in pixels.

durationMillis.title: Duration Millis
durationMillis.description: The duration of the video in milliseconds.

videoMediaMetadata.description: Additional metadata about video media, if available.

viewersCanCopyContent.title: Viewers Can Copy Content
viewersCanCopyContent.description: Whether users with only reader or commenter permission can copy the content of the file. This does not apply to Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

copyRequiresWriterPermission.title: Copy Requires Writer Permission
copyRequiresWriterPermission.description: Whether to require the user to be a writer on the item in order to copy content.

description: Upload a media file to google photos. supports images (up to 200mb) and videos (up to 20gb).

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional description for the media item

file_to_upload.title: FileUploadable
file_to_upload.description: The media file to upload to Google Photos

media_item.title: Media Item
media_item.description: The created media item details returned by the Google Photos API

error.title: Error
error.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

successful.title: Successful
